questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: According to Mendel's Law of Segregation, what happens to the two alleles of a gene during gamete formation?

options.A: Both alleles pass into the same gamete
options.B: They segregate and each gamete receives only one allele
options.C: Alleles fuse before entering the gamete
options.D: Only the dominant allele passes into the gamete

questions.ncert_line: According to the Law of Segregation, the two alleles of a gene segregate during gamete formation and a gamete receives only one of the two factors.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1: Mendel's Laws of Inheritance
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1: Mendel's Laws of Inheritance

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT correctly describe Mendel's Law of Independent Assortment?

options.A: It applies to genes located on different (non-homologous) chromosomes
options.B: Segregation of one pair of alleles is independent of the segregation of another pair
options.C: It can generate new combinations of characters
options.D: It always applies even when two genes are on the same chromosome

questions.ncert_line: The Law of Independent Assortment states that when two pairs of traits are combined in a hybrid, the segregation of one pair of characters is independent of the other pair of characters.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1.3: Law of Independent Assortment
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1.3: Law of Independent Assortment

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which statement about ABO blood groups is INCORRECT?

options.A: IA and IB are codominant alleles
options.B: A person with genotype IAIB has blood group AB
options.C: A person with genotype ii has blood group O
options.D: The IA allele is recessive to the i allele

questions.ncert_line: The three alleles IA, IB and i control the ABO blood groups. IA and IB are codominant and both are dominant over i. A person with genotype ii has blood group O.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.4: Codominance
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.4: Codominance

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In ABO blood group system, a person with genotype IAi will have which blood group?
questions.ncert_line: The three alleles IA, IB and i control the ABO blood groups. IA and IB are codominant and both are dominant over i. A person with genotype ii has blood group O.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.4: Codominance
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.4: Codominance

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about haemophilia is INCORRECT?

options.A: It is an X-linked recessive trait
options.C: Sons of a haemophilic father will always be haemophilic
options.D: Haemophilic daughters are born only when father is haemophilic AND mother is at least a carrier

questions.ncert_line: The sons of a haemophilic father will not be haemophilic since they receive the Y chromosome from father and X chromosome from their mother.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.5: Sex-linked Inheritance
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.5: Sex-linked Inheritance

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Sickle cell anaemia is an example of which type of inheritance?
questions.ncert_line: Sickle cell anaemia is an autosome linked recessive trait. The disease is controlled by a single pair of alleles, HbA and HbS. Only homozygous individuals for HbS (HbS HbS) show the disease.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6: Genetic Disorders
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6: Genetic Disorders

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Individuals with genotype HbA HbS do NOT show sickle cell anaemia but may show sickle cell trait.
Reason (R): Sickle cell anaemia requires homozygosity for the HbS allele.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.ncert_line: Sickle cell anaemia is an autosome linked recessive trait. The disease is controlled by a single pair of alleles, HbA and HbS. Only homozygous individuals for HbS (HbS HbS) show the disease.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6: Genetic Disorders
questions.explanation: Correct answer is A. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6: Genetic Disorders

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Down's syndrome results from trisomy of which chromosome?
questions.ncert_line: Trisomy of chromosome 21 causes Down's syndrome. The affected individual has 47 chromosomes. The chromosomal aberration is called trisomy. The features include a small round head, short stature, and mental retardation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6.2: Chromosomal Disorders
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6.2: Chromosomal Disorders

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The total number of chromosomes in a cell of a Down's syndrome patient is:
questions.ncert_line: Trisomy of chromosome 21 causes Down's syndrome. The affected individual has 47 chromosomes. The chromosomal aberration is called trisomy. The features include a small round head, short stature, and mental retardation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6.2: Chromosomal Disorders
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.6.2: Chromosomal Disorders

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the orientation of the two strands of a DNA double helix?

options.A: Both strands run in the 5'→3' direction
options.B: Both strands run in the 3'→5' direction
options.C: The two strands are antiparallel (one 5'→3', the other 3'→5')
options.D: The orientation of the two strands is the same (parallel)

questions.ncert_line: James Watson and Francis Crick proposed the Double Helix model for the structure of DNA. The two strands of DNA are antiparallel. The bases of complementary strands are held together by hydrogen bonds.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.2: The DNA
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.2: The DNA

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of DNA replication?

options.B: Each daughter DNA has one parental and one new strand
options.C: Both strands serve as templates simultaneously
options.D: It is conservative — the parental strands remain together

questions.ncert_line: DNA replication is semiconservative. The two strands of the parental DNA serve as template for the synthesis of the new complementary strands. Each daughter DNA molecule consists of one parental and one newly synthesised strand.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4: DNA Replication
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4: DNA Replication

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During transcription, which strand of DNA serves as the template?
questions.ncert_line: The strand that has the polarity 3'→5' acts as a template strand. The other strand with polarity 5'→3' is called the coding strand.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.1: Transcription Unit
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.1: Transcription Unit

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following codons does NOT code for any amino acid?
questions.ncert_line: The codon UGA is a stop codon (not coding for any amino acid). AUG is the start codon that codes for methionine. There are 61 sense codons and 3 stop codons (UAA, UAG, UGA).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.4: Genetic Code
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.4: Genetic Code

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: How many stop codons are present in the genetic code?
questions.ncert_line: The codon UGA is a stop codon (not coding for any amino acid). AUG is the start codon that codes for methionine. There are 61 sense codons and 3 stop codons (UAA, UAG, UGA).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.4: Genetic Code
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5.4: Genetic Code

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the lac operon, which of the following is INCORRECT?

options.A: The repressor protein is encoded by the regulator gene
options.B: In the absence of lactose, the repressor binds to the operator
options.C: Lactose acts as an inducer by binding to the repressor
options.D: When lactose binds to the operator, transcription proceeds

questions.ncert_line: In the absence of inducer (lactose), the repressor binds to the operator and blocks transcription. When lactose (inducer) is present, it binds to the repressor, inactivating it, and transcription proceeds.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.7: Regulation of Gene Expression
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.7: Regulation of Gene Expression

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In E. coli, the lac operon is switched ON when:

options.A: Glucose is present and lactose is absent
options.B: Both glucose and lactose are present in equal amounts
options.C: Lactose is present and binds to the repressor protein
options.D: The repressor binds to the promoter region

questions.ncert_line: In the absence of inducer (lactose), the repressor binds to the operator and blocks transcription. When lactose (inducer) is present, it binds to the repressor, inactivating it, and transcription proceeds.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.7: Regulation of Gene Expression
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.7: Regulation of Gene Expression

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: What did Griffith call the chemical substance from heat-killed S-strain bacteria that could transform R-strain bacteria?
questions.ncert_line: Griffith's experiment showed that some chemical from the heat-killed virulent (S) bacteria could transform the non-virulent (R) bacteria into virulent bacteria. This principle was called the transforming principle.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.1: Griffith's Experiment
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.1: Griffith's Experiment

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the Avery-MacLeod-McCarty experiment, transforming activity was destroyed only when treated with:
questions.ncert_line: Avery, MacLeod and McCarty demonstrated that DNA is the transforming principle. They used enzymes to destroy proteins (protease), RNA (RNase), and DNA (DNase) separately. Only DNase destroyed transforming activity.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.2: Search for the Genetic Material
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.2: Search for the Genetic Material

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In the Hershey-Chase experiment, radioactive 32P was used to label which component of bacteriophage T2?
questions.ncert_line: Hershey and Chase worked with T2 bacteriophage to prove that DNA is the genetic material. They grew some viruses on radioactive 35S (labels protein) and some on radioactive 32P (labels DNA).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.3: Hershey and Chase Experiment
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.1.3: Hershey and Chase Experiment

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a condition for Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium?

options.C: High rate of natural selection favouring one allele
options.D: No migration of individuals into or out of the population

questions.ncert_line: Hardy-Weinberg principle states that allele frequencies in a population remain stable and constant from generation to generation — the gene pool remains constant. This is called genetic equilibrium.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.6: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.6: Hardy-Weinberg Principle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Industrial melanism in Biston betularia (peppered moth) is an example of:

options.A: Mutation creating new dark alleles during industrialisation
options.B: Natural selection favouring pre-existing dark-coloured variants

questions.ncert_line: During the industrialisation period in England, the white-winged moths were eliminated by predators and melanised forms (darker forms) were selected. This is an example of natural selection.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.4.1: Darwinian Fitness
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.4.1: Darwinian Fitness

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In angiosperms, triple fusion involves the fusion of one male gamete with:
questions.ncert_line: Double fertilisation is unique to angiosperms. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form the diploid zygote (syngamy) and the other male gamete fuses with the two polar nuclei to form the triploid primary endosperm nucleus (triple fusion).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.7: Double Fertilisation
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.7: Double Fertilisation

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The primary endosperm nucleus formed after triple fusion is:
questions.ncert_line: Double fertilisation is unique to angiosperms. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form the diploid zygote (syngamy) and the other male gamete fuses with the two polar nuclei to form the triploid primary endosperm nucleus (triple fusion).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.7: Double Fertilisation
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.7: Double Fertilisation

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a typical angiosperm embryo sac at maturity?

options.B: It has 3 antipodal cells, 2 synergids, 1 egg cell, and 1 central cell
options.C: The central cell contains 2 polar nuclei
options.D: All 8 nuclei in the embryo sac are contained in 8 separate cells

questions.ncert_line: A typical angiosperm embryo sac at maturity has 7 cells with 8 nuclei — 3 antipodal cells, 2 synergids, 1 egg cell, and 1 large central cell with 2 polar nuclei.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.4.2: Female Gametophyte
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.4.2: Female Gametophyte

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Cells of the tapetum possess dense cytoplasm and generally have more than one nucleus.
Reason (R): The additional nuclei in tapetal cells increase the efficiency of nourishing developing pollen grains.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.ncert_line: The tapetum is the innermost layer of the anther wall. It nourishes the developing pollen grains. Cells of the tapetum have dense cytoplasm and generally more than one nucleus.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.2.1: Microsporogenesis
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.2.1: Microsporogenesis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In angiosperms, how many functional megaspores are typically produced from one megaspore mother cell?
questions.ncert_line: The megaspore mother cell (MMC) is a large cell with a dense cytoplasm. It undergoes meiosis to produce four megaspores. Usually only one megaspore is functional, the others degenerate.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.4.1: Megasporogenesis
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.4.1: Megasporogenesis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which cells undergo meiosis I during spermatogenesis?
questions.ncert_line: Spermatogenesis begins at puberty. The spermatogonia (2n) multiply by mitosis and grow into primary spermatocytes (2n). Each primary spermatocyte undergoes meiosis I to form secondary spermatocytes (n), then meiosis II to form spermatids.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.1: Spermatogenesis
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.1: Spermatogenesis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about oogenesis?

options.A: Primary oocyte is arrested at prophase I
options.B: Meiosis I is completed before ovulation
options.C: Meiosis II is completed after fertilisation
options.D: Meiosis II begins after fertilisation takes place

questions.ncert_line: Oogenesis begins during fetal development. The oogonia start meiosis but are arrested at prophase I (primary oocyte). Meiosis I completes just before ovulation. Meiosis II is completed only after fertilisation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.3: Oogenesis
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.3: Oogenesis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hormone surge triggers ovulation in the human menstrual cycle?
questions.ncert_line: The menstrual cycle consists of menstruation (days 1-5), the follicular/proliferative phase (days 6-13), ovulation (day 14), and the luteal/secretory phase (days 15-28). LH surge triggers ovulation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.4: Menstrual Cycle
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.4: Menstrual Cycle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first menstruation beginning at puberty is called:
questions.ncert_line: The first menstruation begins at puberty and is called menarche. The cyclical menstruation continues till menopause.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.4: Menstrual Cycle
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2.4: Menstrual Cycle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The volume of air inspired or expired during a normal respiration (tidal volume) is approximately:
questions.ncert_line: Tidal Volume (TV) is the volume of air inspired or expired during normal respiration — about 500 mL. Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV) is about 2500-3000 mL. Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV) is about 1000-1100 mL.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.3: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.3: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Vital capacity of lungs is the sum of:

options.B: Tidal volume + Inspiratory reserve volume + Expiratory reserve volume
options.D: Both B and C are correct

questions.ncert_line: Tidal Volume (TV) is the volume of air inspired or expired during normal respiration — about 500 mL. Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV) is about 2500-3000 mL. Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV) is about 1000-1100 mL.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.3: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.3: Respiratory Volumes and Capacities

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which part of the nephron is impermeable to water but allows active transport of electrolytes?

options.A: Descending limb of loop of Henle
options.C: Ascending limb of loop of Henle

questions.ncert_line: The ascending limb of loop of Henle is impermeable to water but allows active transport of electrolytes. This creates the concentration gradient in the medulla. The descending limb is permeable to water.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and Their Elimination, Section 19.2.1: Tubular Reabsorption
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and Their Elimination, Section 19.2.1: Tubular Reabsorption

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: ADH (vasopressin) is synthesised in the hypothalamus but released from:
questions.ncert_line: ADH (antidiuretic hormone/vasopressin) and oxytocin are synthesised in the hypothalamus but stored and released from the neurohypophysis (posterior pituitary).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.2: Pituitary Gland
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.2: Pituitary Gland

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hormone stimulates the secretion of gastric juice (HCl and pepsinogen) from gastric glands?
questions.ncert_line: Gastrin stimulates the gastric glands to secrete HCl and pepsinogen. Secretin stimulates the pancreas to secrete bicarbonate ions. Cholecystokinin (CCK) causes the release of bile from the gall bladder.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 16 — Digestion and Absorption, Section 16.3.3: Hormones of GI Tract
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 16 — Digestion and Absorption, Section 16.3.3: Hormones of GI Tract

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The junction between two neurons across which nerve impulses are transmitted is called a:
questions.ncert_line: An impulse is transmitted from neuron to neuron across a synapse. At a chemical synapse, the electrical signal is converted to a chemical signal. Neurotransmitters are released from the presynaptic terminal into the synaptic cleft.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 21 — Neural Control and Coordination, Section 21.2.2: Synapse
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 21 — Neural Control and Coordination, Section 21.2.2: Synapse

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which structure in bacteria helps them attach to rocks and to host tissues?
questions.ncert_line: Prokaryotic cells lack membrane-bound organelles. The genetic material (DNA) is not enveloped by a nuclear membrane. The ribosome of prokaryotes is 70S (50S + 30S). Fimbriae help bacteria attach to rocks and host tissue.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.2: Prokaryotic Cells
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.2: Prokaryotic Cells

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following has 70S ribosomes?
questions.ncert_line: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis. In eukaryotes, the cytoplasmic ribosomes are 80S (60S + 40S). Mitochondria and chloroplasts contain 70S ribosomes (50S + 30S), similar to prokaryotic ribosomes.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.5.10: Ribosomes
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.5.10: Ribosomes

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: At which sub-stage of prophase I does crossing over (recombination) occur?
questions.ncert_line: Prophase I of meiosis is subdivided into leptotene, zygotene, pachytene, diplotene and diakinesis. Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes) occurs during zygotene. Crossing over (recombination) occurs at pachytene.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3.1: Meiosis I
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3.1: Meiosis I

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes with formation of synaptonemal complex) occurs at which sub-stage of prophase I?
questions.ncert_line: Prophase I of meiosis is subdivided into leptotene, zygotene, pachytene, diplotene and diakinesis. Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes) occurs during zygotene. Crossing over (recombination) occurs at pachytene.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3.1: Meiosis I
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3.1: Meiosis I

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: DNA replication occurs during which phase of the cell cycle?
questions.ncert_line: Cell cycle consists of two phases: interphase (G1, S, G2) and M phase (mitosis). DNA synthesis occurs during the S phase. The G1 and G2 phases are gap phases involving intense metabolic activity.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.1: Cell Cycle
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.1: Cell Cycle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: During which stage of mitosis do centromeres split and chromatids begin moving to opposite poles?
questions.ncert_line: Mitosis is divided into four stages: prophase, metaphase, anaphase and telophase. During anaphase, centromeres split and the chromatids move toward opposite poles pulled by spindle fibres.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.2: M Phase
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.2: M Phase

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which unit is used to express the productivity of an ecosystem?
questions.ncert_line: The rate of production of organic matter during photosynthesis is called Gross Primary Productivity (GPP). Net Primary Productivity (NPP) = GPP - Respiration. It is expressed in terms of g m-2 yr-1 or kcal m-2 yr-1.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.2: Productivity
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.2: Productivity

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Net Primary Productivity (NPP) of an ecosystem is:
questions.ncert_line: The rate of production of organic matter during photosynthesis is called Gross Primary Productivity (GPP). Net Primary Productivity (NPP) = GPP - Respiration. It is expressed in terms of g m-2 yr-1 or kcal m-2 yr-1.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.2: Productivity
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.2: Productivity

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is considered the most important cause driving species to extinction according to the 'Evil Quartet'?
questions.ncert_line: Habitat loss and fragmentation is the most important cause driving species to extinction. The other members of The Evil Quartet are: over-exploitation, alien species invasions, and co-extinctions.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.3: Loss of Biodiversity
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.3: Loss of Biodiversity

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which interaction does one organism benefit while the other is neither benefited nor harmed?
questions.ncert_line: In mutualism both species benefit (+/+). In commensalism one species benefits and the other is unaffected (+/0). In parasitism one benefits at the expense of the other (+/-). In competition both are negatively affected (-/-).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3: Population Interactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3: Population Interactions

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: An epiphyte growing on a mango tree branch is an example of:
questions.ncert_line: In mutualism both species benefit (+/+). In commensalism one species benefits and the other is unaffected (+/0). In parasitism one benefits at the expense of the other (+/-). In competition both are negatively affected (-/-).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3: Population Interactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3: Population Interactions

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following ecological pyramids is ALWAYS upright (never inverted)?
questions.ncert_line: A pyramid of numbers can be inverted (as in a tree ecosystem where one tree supports many insects). A pyramid of biomass can also be inverted (in an aquatic ecosystem). However, the pyramid of energy is always upright.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.4: Ecological Pyramids
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.4: Ecological Pyramids

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The reaction centre of Photosystem I has an absorption maxima at:
questions.ncert_line: In PS I, the reaction centre chlorophyll a has an absorption peak at 700 nm and is called P700. In PS II, the reaction centre chlorophyll a has an absorption peak at 680 nm and is called P680.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4: Light Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4: Light Reactions

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: P680, the reaction centre of PS II, absorbs light at 680 nm. It is called P680 because:

options.C: It absorbs maximally at 680 nm wavelength

questions.ncert_line: In PS I, the reaction centre chlorophyll a has an absorption peak at 700 nm and is called P700. In PS II, the reaction centre chlorophyll a has an absorption peak at 680 nm and is called P680.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4: Light Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4: Light Reactions

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about C4 plants?

options.A: The first stable product of CO2 fixation is oxaloacetic acid (OAA)
options.B: CO2 fixation first occurs in mesophyll cells
options.C: Rubisco is absent in C4 plants
options.D: The bundle sheath cells carry out the Calvin cycle

questions.ncert_line: In C3 plants, the first stable product of carbon fixation is 3-phosphoglyceric acid (3-PGA), a 3-carbon compound. In C4 plants, the first stable product is oxaloacetic acid (OAA), a 4-carbon compound, formed in mesophyll cells.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.5.2: C4 Pathway
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.5.2: C4 Pathway

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which protein is considered the most abundant in the biosphere?
questions.ncert_line: The enzyme Rubisco (ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase-oxygenase) is the most abundant protein in the world. It catalyses the first step of carbon fixation by combining CO2 with RuBP.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.5.1: Calvin Cycle
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.5.1: Calvin Cycle

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correctly matched with its primary function?
questions.ncert_line: Cytokinins promote cell division. Abscisic acid (ABA) promotes seed dormancy and stomatal closure. Ethylene is a gaseous plant hormone that promotes fruit ripening and abscission. Gibberellins promote stem elongation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4: Plant Growth Regulators
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4: Plant Growth Regulators

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which phytohormone is a gas at room temperature?
questions.ncert_line: Cytokinins promote cell division. Abscisic acid (ABA) promotes seed dormancy and stomatal closure. Ethylene is a gaseous plant hormone that promotes fruit ripening and abscission. Gibberellins promote stem elongation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4: Plant Growth Regulators
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4: Plant Growth Regulators

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The technique used to amplify a specific segment of DNA in vitro is:
questions.ncert_line: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) amplifies a specific segment of DNA. It involves denaturation (94°C), annealing of primers (~55°C), and extension by Taq polymerase (72°C). Each cycle doubles the amount of target DNA.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.2.1: PCR
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.2.1: PCR

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: EcoRI is a restriction endonuclease that recognises and cuts the sequence 5'GAATTC3'. What type of ends does it produce?
questions.ncert_line: Restriction endonucleases recognise specific palindromic nucleotide sequences in DNA and cut them. EcoRI recognises the sequence 5'GAATTC3' and cuts between G and A. They produce sticky ends which facilitate cloning.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.1.1: Restriction Enzymes
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.1.1: Restriction Enzymes

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about agarose gel electrophoresis is INCORRECT?

options.A: DNA fragments migrate toward the anode
options.B: Smaller fragments move faster and travel farther from the wells
options.C: DNA is stained with ethidium bromide and visualised under UV light
options.D: Larger DNA fragments are found closer to the positive electrode (anode)

questions.ncert_line: In agarose gel electrophoresis, DNA fragments migrate toward the anode (positive electrode) since DNA is negatively charged. Smaller fragments migrate faster and are found farther from the wells. DNA is stained with ethidium bromide.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.2.2: Gel Electrophoresis
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.2.2: Gel Electrophoresis

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bt cotton is resistant to which type of pest?
questions.ncert_line: Bt toxin genes (cry genes) from Bacillus thuringiensis are incorporated into the cotton plant (Bt cotton). The cry genes encode Cry proteins (Bt toxin) which are toxic to lepidopteran insects (cotton bollworm).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and Its Applications, Section 12.2.1: Bt Cotton
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and Its Applications, Section 12.2.1: Bt Cotton

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first clinical gene therapy (1990) was given for deficiency of which enzyme?
questions.ncert_line: The first clinical gene therapy was given in 1990 to a 4-year old girl with adenosine deaminase (ADA) deficiency. Functional ADA cDNA was introduced into her lymphocytes. ADA deficiency can also be cured by bone marrow transplantation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and Its Applications, Section 12.3: Gene Therapy
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and Its Applications, Section 12.3: Gene Therapy

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which group of organisms are the cell walls made of two thin overlapping silica shells that fit together like a soap box?
questions.ncert_line: Chrysophytes (diatoms and golden algae) have cell walls made of two thin overlapping shells that fit together like a soap box. The cell walls are embedded with silica.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.3: Kingdom Protista
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.3: Kingdom Protista

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question:
Assertion (A): All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates.
Reason (R): Vertebrates possess a notochord only during the embryonic period, which is later replaced by a cartilaginous or bony vertebral column.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.ncert_line: The notochord is replaced by a cartilaginous or bony vertebral column in the adult vertebrate. Thus all vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2: Phylum Chordata
questions.explanation: Correct answer is A. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2: Phylum Chordata

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gemmae are structures found in bryophytes that help in:
questions.ncert_line: In bryophytes, the main plant body is a gametophyte (haploid). The sporophyte is dependent on the gametophyte for nutrition. Gemmae are green multicellular asexual buds found in gemma cups, helping in vegetative reproduction.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.2: Bryophytes
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.2: Bryophytes

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In which of the following plants do the male and female gametophytes NOT have an independent free-living existence?
questions.ncert_line: In gymnosperms, the male and female gametophytes do not have an independent free-living existence. They are totally dependent on the sporophyte. Cedrus is an example of gymnosperm.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.4: Gymnosperms
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.4: Gymnosperms

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about antibody structure?

options.A: Each antibody molecule has two light chains and two heavy chains
options.B: The heavy and light chains are held together by disulfide bonds
options.C: The antigen binding site is located at the C-terminal region of both chains
options.D: The constant region of the chains determines effector functions

questions.ncert_line: In an antibody molecule, the antigen binding site is located at the N-terminal region of both heavy and light chains (variable region). The constant region determines effector function.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2.2: Antibody
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2.2: Antibody

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Immature lymphocytes differentiate into antigen-sensitive lymphocytes in:

options.B: Primary lymphoid organs (thymus and bone marrow)

questions.ncert_line: The thymus and bone marrow are the primary lymphoid organs where immature lymphocytes differentiate into antigen-sensitive lymphocytes. Secondary lymphoid organs include the spleen, lymph nodes, and tonsils.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2.1: Immune System
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2.1: Immune System

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions, the molar conductivity at infinite dilution (Λ°m) of a weak electrolyte can be calculated from:

options.A: Direct measurement at very low concentrations
options.B: Sum of the limiting molar conductivities of its constituent ions
options.C: The product of limiting molar conductivities of its constituent ions
options.D: Extrapolation of concentration vs conductivity graph

questions.ncert_line: According to Kohlrausch's law, at infinite dilution, each ion makes a definite contribution to the molar conductivity of an electrolyte, irrespective of the nature of the other ion.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.3: Kohlrausch's Law
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.3: Kohlrausch's Law

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a first order reaction, the half-life (t₁/₂) is:

options.D: Proportional to the square of initial concentration

questions.ncert_line: For a first order reaction, the integrated rate equation is: k = (2.303/t) log ([R]₀/[R]). The half-life of a first order reaction is: t₁/₂ = 0.693/k. It is independent of initial concentration.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: If the half-life of a first order reaction is 1 minute, the time required for 99.9% completion is approximately:
questions.ncert_line: For a first order reaction, the integrated rate equation is: k = (2.303/t) log ([R]₀/[R]). The half-life of a first order reaction is: t₁/₂ = 0.693/k. It is independent of initial concentration.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes D-fructose?

options.B: A ketohexose that is laevorotatory, found in honey
options.C: An aldohexose that reduces Fehling's solution
options.D: A disaccharide formed by glucose and galactose

questions.ncert_line: Fructose is a ketohexose. It is found in honey and fruits. It is laevorotatory and exists in α and β anomeric forms due to the presence of an anomeric carbon.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.1.2: Monosaccharides
questions.explanation: Correct answer is B. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.1.2: Monosaccharides

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the correct decreasing order of basic strength?
questions.ncert_line: The basic character of amines depends on the availability of the lone pair of electrons on nitrogen. Aliphatic amines are more basic than aromatic amines due to the delocalization of the lone pair in aromatic amines.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.4: Chemical Reactions of Amines
questions.explanation: Correct answer is D. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.4: Chemical Reactions of Amines

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a plane electromagnetic wave, the electric field E and magnetic field B are:

options.A: Parallel to each other and to the direction of propagation
options.B: Perpendicular to each other but parallel to the direction of propagation
options.C: Perpendicular to each other and both perpendicular to the direction of propagation
options.D: Parallel to each other and perpendicular to direction of propagation

questions.ncert_line: Electromagnetic waves are transverse waves. The speed of EM waves in vacuum is c = 3×10⁸ m/s. E and B are perpendicular to each other and also perpendicular to the direction of propagation.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3: Electromagnetic Spectrum
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3: Electromagnetic Spectrum

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the photoelectric effect, increasing the intensity of incident light (keeping frequency constant above threshold) will:

options.A: Increase the maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons
options.C: Increase the number of photoelectrons emitted per second

questions.ncert_line: Einstein explained the photoelectric effect using the concept of photons. The maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons is: Kmax = hν - φ. The stopping potential depends on frequency and not on intensity of light.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter, Section 11.3: Photoelectric Effect
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter, Section 11.3: Photoelectric Effect

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to Bohr's model, the angular momentum of an electron in the nth orbit is:
questions.ncert_line: In the Bohr model, the electron revolves around the nucleus only in certain stable orbits (stationary states) for which the angular momentum is a whole number multiple of h/2π (L = nh/2π).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.2: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.2: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The degree of dissociation (α) of a weak electrolyte can be calculated using the formula α = Λm/Λ°m. What does Λ°m represent?

options.A: Molar conductivity at a given concentration
options.B: Limiting molar conductivity at infinite dilution

questions.ncert_line: The degree of dissociation (α) of a weak electrolyte is equal to the ratio of its molar conductivity at a given concentration (Λm) to its limiting molar conductivity (Λ°m): α = Λm / Λ°m.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.4: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.4: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The molar conductivity (Λm) of a 0.050 mol L⁻¹ solution of a monobasic weak acid is 90 S cm² mol⁻¹ and Λ°m is 400 S cm² mol⁻¹. What is the degree of dissociation?
questions.ncert_line: The degree of dissociation (α) of a weak electrolyte is equal to the ratio of its molar conductivity at a given concentration (Λm) to its limiting molar conductivity (Λ°m): α = Λm / Λ°m.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.4: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.4: Conductance of Electrolytic Solutions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a galvanic cell?

options.B: The anode is the negative terminal
options.D: The anode is the positive terminal

questions.ncert_line: In a galvanic (voltaic) cell, the electrode where oxidation occurs is the anode (negative terminal) and the electrode where reduction occurs is the cathode (positive terminal). The cell EMF = E°cathode − E°anode.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.3.1: Standard Electrode Potential
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.3.1: Standard Electrode Potential

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Why can the molar conductivity at infinite dilution (Λ°m) for weak electrolytes NOT be determined by direct measurement?

options.A: Weak electrolytes decompose at infinite dilution
options.C: The curve does not show a clear limiting value when extrapolated
options.D: Weak electrolytes form precipitates at very low concentrations

questions.ncert_line: Kohlrausch's law allows calculation of Λ°m for weak electrolytes which cannot be obtained by direct measurement. For CH3COOH: Λ°m(CH3COOH) = Λ°m(CH3COONa) + Λ°m(HCl) − Λ°m(NaCl).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.3: Kohlrausch's Law
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4.3: Kohlrausch's Law

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Faraday's second law of electrolysis, the amounts of different substances deposited by the same quantity of electricity are proportional to their:
questions.ncert_line: Faraday's first law: The amount of chemical reaction at an electrode is proportional to the quantity of electricity passed. Faraday's second law: The amounts of different substances deposited by the same quantity of electricity are proportional to their equivalent masses.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.2: Electrolytic Cells and Electrolysis
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.2: Electrolytic Cells and Electrolysis

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following statements about order and molecularity is INCORRECT?

options.A: Order of a reaction can be zero or fractional
options.B: Molecularity is always a whole number
options.D: Molecularity can be zero for unimolecular reactions

questions.ncert_line: Order of a reaction is determined experimentally and can be zero, fractional or integer. Molecularity is the number of reacting species in an elementary reaction and is always a whole number. Molecularity cannot be zero or fractional.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.1.2: Order and Molecularity
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.1.2: Order and Molecularity

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life (t₁/₂):

options.B: Is directly proportional to initial concentration
options.C: Is inversely proportional to initial concentration

questions.ncert_line: For a zero order reaction, the rate is independent of concentration: rate = k. The integrated equation is [R] = [R]₀ − kt. The half-life is t₁/₂ = [R]₀/2k — it depends on initial concentration.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.1: Zero Order Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.1: Zero Order Reactions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following processes follows first order kinetics?

options.A: Decomposition of HI on a gold surface (zero order)
options.C: Combination of H2 and I2 to form HI
options.D: Decomposition of N2O5 (which is second order)

questions.ncert_line: For a first order reaction: k = (2.303/t) log([R]₀/[R]). The half-life t₁/₂ = 0.693/k is independent of initial concentration. All radioactive decays follow first order kinetics.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.2.2: First Order Reactions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to the Arrhenius equation, a plot of log k vs 1/T gives a straight line. The slope of this line is:
questions.ncert_line: The Arrhenius equation k = Ae^(−Ea/RT) shows that rate constant increases with temperature. The pre-exponential factor A (frequency factor) accounts for collision frequency. A plot of ln k vs 1/T gives slope = −Ea/R.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.4: Temperature Dependence
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.4: Temperature Dependence

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to collision theory, the fraction of molecules that actually react upon collision is low because:

options.A: Most molecules move too slowly to collide
options.B: Most collisions lack sufficient energy AND proper orientation
options.C: The activation energy barrier does not exist for most reactions
options.D: Collision frequency is very low at room temperature

questions.ncert_line: According to collision theory, reactant molecules must collide with sufficient energy (≥ activation energy) and with proper orientation for a reaction to occur. Not every collision leads to a reaction.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.5: Collision Theory
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.5: Collision Theory

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A solution shows negative deviation from Raoult's law. Which of the following is NOT true for such a solution?

options.A: The vapour pressure of the solution is lower than predicted by Raoult's law
options.C: The interactions between unlike molecules are stronger than like-molecule interactions

questions.ncert_line: Raoult's law: For a solution of volatile liquids, the partial vapour pressure of each component is directly proportional to its mole fraction: pA = x_A × p°A. An ideal solution obeys Raoult's law at all compositions.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4: Vapour Pressure of Liquid Solutions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4: Vapour Pressure of Liquid Solutions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
questions.ncert_line: Colligative properties depend only on the number of solute particles, not their nature. They include: relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation of boiling point, depression of freezing point, and osmotic pressure.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5: Colligative Properties
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5: Colligative Properties

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which aqueous solution would exhibit the highest boiling point?
questions.ncert_line: The Van't Hoff factor (i) accounts for dissociation or association of solutes. For electrolytes that dissociate, i > 1. For solutes that associate (e.g., acetic acid in benzene), i < 1. For non-electrolytes, i = 1.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5.5: Abnormal Molar Masses
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5.5: Abnormal Molar Masses

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Henry's law states that the solubility of a gas in a liquid at constant temperature is:

options.A: Inversely proportional to the partial pressure of the gas
options.B: Directly proportional to the partial pressure of the gas
options.C: Independent of the partial pressure of the gas
options.D: Proportional to the square of partial pressure

questions.ncert_line: Henry's law states that the solubility of a gas in a liquid at constant temperature is directly proportional to the partial pressure of the gas above the solution: p = KH × x (where KH is Henry's constant).
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.3: Solubility of Gases in Liquids
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.3: Solubility of Gases in Liquids

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Two solutions of equal osmotic pressure at the same temperature are called:
questions.ncert_line: Osmotic pressure (π) = iCRT, where C is molarity, R is gas constant, T is temperature in Kelvin, and i is Van't Hoff factor. Osmotic pressure is a colligative property.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5.4: Osmosis and Osmotic Pressure
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.5.4: Osmosis and Osmotic Pressure

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N₂(g) + O₂(g) ⇌ 2NO(g), ΔH = +180.7 kJ mol⁻¹. Higher yield of NO is obtained by:

options.B: Higher temperature; pressure has no effect (Δn = 0)

questions.ncert_line: Le Chatelier's principle: If a system at equilibrium is disturbed by a change in concentration, temperature or pressure, the equilibrium will shift in the direction that tends to counteract the disturbance.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.7: Le Chatelier's Principle
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.7: Le Chatelier's Principle

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction PCl₅(g) ⇌ PCl₃(g) + Cl₂(g), the relationship between Kp and Kc at temperature T is:
questions.ncert_line: The relationship between Kp and Kc is: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn, where Δn is the difference in moles of gaseous products and reactants. When Δn = 0, Kp = Kc.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.5: Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.5: Equilibrium Constant

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: At 298 K, the value of the ionic product of water (Kw) is:
questions.ncert_line: The ionic product of water (Kw) = [H+][OH−] = 1.0 × 10⁻¹⁴ at 298 K. In a neutral solution, [H+] = [OH−] = 1.0 × 10⁻⁷ M. At temperatures above 298 K, Kw increases.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.11.1: Ionisation of Water
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.11.1: Ionisation of Water

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: An acidic buffer solution is prepared by mixing:

options.A: A strong acid and its salt with a strong base
options.B: A weak acid and its salt with a strong base
options.C: A weak base and its salt with a strong acid
options.D: Equal volumes of a strong acid and strong base

questions.ncert_line: A buffer solution resists changes in pH on addition of small amounts of acid or base. An acidic buffer is a mixture of a weak acid and its salt with a strong base (e.g., CH3COOH/CH3COONa). Its pH = pKa + log([salt]/[acid]).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.11.4: Buffer Solutions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.11.4: Buffer Solutions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A process is spontaneous at constant temperature and pressure when:
questions.ncert_line: Gibbs energy: G = H − TS. For a spontaneous process at constant T and P, ΔG < 0. At equilibrium, ΔG = 0. The relationship between ΔG° and equilibrium constant K is: ΔG° = −RT ln K.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.8: Gibbs Energy and Spontaneity
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.8: Gibbs Energy and Spontaneity

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following processes results in an INCREASE in entropy?

options.A: Condensation of water vapour to liquid
options.B: Crystallisation of a salt from solution
options.D: Dissolution of NaCl in water does NOT increase entropy

questions.ncert_line: Entropy (S) is a measure of the degree of disorder or randomness of a system. The entropy of the universe increases for all spontaneous processes (ΔS_universe > 0). Entropy of a pure crystalline substance at 0 K is zero (Third Law).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.7: Entropy
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.7: Entropy

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Hess's law is a consequence of which law of thermodynamics?
questions.ncert_line: Hess's law states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is the same whether it occurs in one step or in several steps. ΔH°reaction = ΣΔH°f(products) − ΣΔH°f(reactants).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.6.1: Hess's Law
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.6.1: Hess's Law

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which set of quantum numbers is NOT possible for an electron?
questions.ncert_line: Four quantum numbers describe an electron: principal (n), azimuthal (l, 0 to n−1), magnetic (ml, −l to +l), and spin (ms, +½ or −½). No two electrons in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers (Pauli's exclusion principle).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6: Quantum Mechanical Model
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6: Quantum Mechanical Model

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The de Broglie wavelength of a particle with momentum p is:
questions.ncert_line: de Broglie proposed that matter has wave-particle duality. The de Broglie wavelength: λ = h/mv = h/p, where h is Planck's constant and mv is momentum. This applies to all matter but is significant only for very small particles.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.5.1: de Broglie Relationship
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.5.1: de Broglie Relationship

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Heisenberg's uncertainty principle states that it is impossible to simultaneously determine precisely:

options.A: The mass and velocity of a particle
options.B: The position and momentum of a subatomic particle
options.C: The energy and temperature of a system
options.D: The charge and mass of an electron

questions.ncert_line: Heisenberg's uncertainty principle: It is impossible to simultaneously determine precisely both the position and momentum of a subatomic particle: Δx × Δp ≥ h/4π.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.5.2: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.5.2: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Statement I: A hypothetical diatomic molecule with bond order zero does not exist (is unstable).
Statement II: As bond order increases, bond length decreases.

options.A: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
options.D: Both Statement I and Statement II are false

questions.ncert_line: Bond order is the number of bonds between a pair of atoms. As bond order increases, bond length decreases and bond enthalpy increases. A molecule with bond order zero does not exist (is unstable).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.5: Molecular Orbital Theory
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.5: Molecular Orbital Theory

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to VSEPR theory, which repulsion is the strongest?
questions.ncert_line: VSEPR theory: The shape of a molecule is determined by the repulsion between electron pairs (bonding and lone pairs) around the central atom. Lone pair-lone pair repulsion > lone pair-bond pair > bond pair-bond pair.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.3: VSEPR Theory
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.3: VSEPR Theory

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The hybridisation of carbon in ethyne (HC≡CH) is:
questions.ncert_line: In sp3 hybridisation, one s and three p orbitals mix to form four equivalent sp3 hybrid orbitals directed toward the corners of a tetrahedron. In sp2, three equivalent orbitals form a trigonal planar arrangement.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.7: Hybridisation
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.7: Hybridisation

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following CANNOT form intermolecular hydrogen bonds?
questions.ncert_line: Hydrogen bond forms between highly electronegative atoms (F, O, N) and hydrogen bonded to another electronegative atom. Intermolecular H-bonding raises boiling point. Intramolecular H-bonding (as in o-nitrophenol) lowers boiling point.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.9: Hydrogen Bonding
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure, Section 4.9: Hydrogen Bonding

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The first ionisation enthalpy of oxygen (O) is lower than that of nitrogen (N). The correct reason is:

options.A: Oxygen has more electrons than nitrogen
options.B: Nitrogen has a stable half-filled 2p subshell (2p³) making it harder to remove an electron
options.C: Oxygen has a larger atomic radius than nitrogen
options.D: The 2p orbital of nitrogen is higher in energy than that of oxygen

questions.ncert_line: Ionisation enthalpy generally increases across a period (left to right) and decreases down a group. There are exceptions: IE of Be > B (B has a 2p electron which is easier to remove than 2s) and IE of N > O (N has a stable half-filled 2p).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements and Periodicity, Section 3.7: Periodic Trends
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements and Periodicity, Section 3.7: Periodic Trends

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: On the Pauling scale, which of the following is the correct comparison of electronegativities of N and Cl?

options.A: N and Cl have the same electronegativity (both = 3.0)
options.B: N (3.0) is less electronegative than Cl (3.2)
options.C: N (3.5) is more electronegative than Cl (3.2)
options.D: Cl is more electronegative than F

questions.ncert_line: Electronegativity increases across a period and decreases down a group. Fluorine (F) is the most electronegative element. The electronegativity of N and Cl are different on the Pauling scale (N = 3.0, Cl = 3.2).
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements and Periodicity, Section 3.7.3: Electronegativity
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements and Periodicity, Section 3.7.3: Electronegativity

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Markovnikov's rule, when HBr adds to propene (CH₃-CH=CH₂), the bromine attaches to:

options.A: C1 (terminal carbon with more H atoms)
options.D: The π bond breaks and Br adds to both carbons

questions.ncert_line: Markovnikov's rule: In the addition of HX to an unsymmetrical alkene, the hydrogen attaches to the carbon with more hydrogen atoms (the less substituted carbon) and the halide attaches to the more substituted carbon.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.2: Addition Reactions of Alkenes
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.2: Addition Reactions of Alkenes

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Anti-Markovnikov addition of HBr to propene occurs in the presence of peroxides. This mechanism involves:
questions.ncert_line: In the presence of peroxides (ROOR), HBr adds to alkenes in an anti-Markovnikov fashion (free radical addition). The bromine attaches to the less substituted carbon. This only applies to HBr, NOT HCl or HI.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.2: Free Radical Addition
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.2: Free Radical Addition

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Benzene has 6 π electrons. According to Hückel's rule (4n+2), this corresponds to n =
questions.ncert_line: For aromaticity, a compound must be: (1) cyclic, (2) planar, (3) fully conjugated (each atom contributes a p-orbital), and (4) contain 4n+2 π electrons (Hückel's rule, n = 0, 1, 2...). Benzene (6 π electrons, n=1) is the classic example.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.4.2: Aromaticity
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.4.2: Aromaticity

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following can show cis-trans (geometric) isomerism?
questions.ncert_line: Cis-trans (geometric) isomerism occurs in alkenes when each carbon of the double bond has two different substituents, OR in cyclic compounds when substituents are on different carbons of the ring. 1,2-dimethylcyclohexane shows cis-trans isomerism.
questions.ncert_section: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.4: Geometrical Isomerism
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3.4: Geometrical Isomerism

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: SN2 reactions proceed with which stereochemical outcome?
questions.ncert_line: SN1 reactions proceed via a carbocation intermediate (two steps). They are favoured by tertiary substrates and polar protic solvents. SN2 reactions are concerted (one step) and proceed with inversion of configuration (Walden inversion). They are favoured by primary substrates.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3.1: Nucleophilic Substitution
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3.1: Nucleophilic Substitution

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question:
Assertion (A): Ethyl iodide undergoes SN2 reaction faster than ethyl chloride.
Reason (R): Iodide is a better leaving group than chloride due to its larger size and weaker C−I bond.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.ncert_line: A good leaving group is one that can carry away the electron pair and form a stable species. The order of leaving group ability: I⁻ > Br⁻ > Cl⁻ > F⁻. Iodide is the best leaving group because of its large size and weak C−I bond.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3: Reactions of Haloalkanes
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3: Reactions of Haloalkanes

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: According to Saytzeff's rule, the predominant product of dehydrohalogenation is:

options.D: A mixture of equal amounts of all possible alkenes

questions.ncert_line: Saytzeff's rule states that in elimination reactions, the predominant alkene formed is the more substituted (more stable) one. Dehydrohalogenation of 2-bromobutane gives but-2-ene (major) rather than but-1-ene.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3.2: Elimination Reactions
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3.2: Elimination Reactions

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Phenol is more acidic than ethanol because:

options.A: Phenol has a higher molecular weight
options.B: The phenoxide ion is stabilised by resonance delocalization into the benzene ring
options.C: Phenol has an −OH group attached to an aromatic ring
options.D: The O−H bond in phenol is weaker than in ethanol

questions.ncert_line: Phenol is more acidic than alcohol (pKa ~10) because the phenoxide ion is stabilised by resonance delocalization of the negative charge into the benzene ring. Phenol undergoes electrophilic substitution preferentially at ortho and para positions.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 11 — Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers, Section 11.3.2: Reactions of Phenols
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 11 — Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers, Section 11.3.2: Reactions of Phenols

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The correct decreasing order of acidity among the following aliphatic acids is:

options.D: All four acids have equal acidity

questions.ncert_line: The acidity of carboxylic acids decreases with increasing alkyl chain length (electron-donating inductive effect). The order: HCOOH > CH3COOH > CH3CH2COOH. Electron-withdrawing groups on the α-carbon increase acidity.
questions.ncert_section: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5.2: Reactions of Carboxylic Acids
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5.2: Reactions of Carboxylic Acids

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aldol condensation requires a carbonyl compound with at least one:
questions.ncert_line: In the aldol condensation, an aldehyde or ketone with an α-hydrogen reacts with another carbonyl compound in the presence of base to give a β-hydroxy carbonyl compound (aldol). On heating, dehydration occurs to give an α,β-unsaturated compound.
