questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of release of ovum from the Graafian follicle is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Ovulation is the release of the secondary oocyte (ovum) from the Graafian follicle, triggered by LH surge.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ovulation = release of ovum from Graafian follicle. Occurs around day 14 of 28-day cycle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The hormone responsible for maintaining the corpus luteum during early pregnancy is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: hCG (human chorionic gonadotropin) secreted by the trophoblast maintains corpus luteum during early pregnancy.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. hCG maintains corpus luteum → corpus luteum secretes progesterone → maintains uterine lining.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In gel electrophoresis, DNA fragments are separated based on:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.3
questions.ncert_line: DNA fragments migrate in agarose gel based on size — smaller fragments move faster toward positive electrode.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Gel electrophoresis separates by size. Smaller = faster. Stained with ethidium bromide.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bt toxin produced by Bacillus thuringiensis is a:

options.B: Protoxin (inactive form) activated in insect gut

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.1
questions.ncert_line: Bt toxin exists as inactive protoxin in bacteria. The alkaline pH of insect gut activates it by solubilising.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Bt toxin is a protoxin — inactive in bacteria, activated by alkaline insect midgut pH.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The first transgenic cow produced to give human protein in its milk was:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Rosie was the first transgenic cow that produced human protein-enriched milk (human alpha-lactalbumin).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Rosie — first transgenic cow with human alpha-lactalbumin in milk. Dolly was cloned sheep.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Gene therapy was first used to treat:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: The first clinical gene therapy was used in 1990 for ADA deficiency (SCID) — lymphocytes were given functional ADA gene.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. First gene therapy — ADA deficiency (1990). ADA enzyme essential for immune function.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The drug colchicine inhibits cell division by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Colchicine binds to tubulin and prevents polymerisation of spindle fibres — arrests mitosis at metaphase.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Colchicine inhibits spindle formation → arrests cell division at metaphase. Used to make polyploids.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Streptokinase used as a clot buster is produced by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Streptokinase is produced by Streptococcus bacteria. It is used as a thrombolytic drug to dissolve blood clots.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Streptokinase from Streptococcus. Used to treat myocardial infarction (heart attack).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following microbes is NOT correctly matched with its product?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: Acetic acid (vinegar) is produced by Acetobacter aceti, not Lactobacillus. Lactobacillus produces lactic acid.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Lactobacillus → lactic acid. Acetic acid → Acetobacter aceti.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cyclosporin A, an immunosuppressant used in organ transplants, is produced by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Cyclosporin A is produced by the fungus Trichoderma polysporum. Used to prevent organ rejection.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Cyclosporin A — Trichoderma polysporum. Statins (cholesterol-lowering) — Monascus purpureus.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of radial nodes in a 3p orbital is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Number of radial nodes = n − l − 1 = 3 − 1 − 1 = 1 for 3p orbital.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Radial nodes = n − l − 1 = 3−1−1 = 1. Angular nodes = l = 1 for p orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which quantum number determines the shape of an orbital?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Azimuthal quantum number l determines orbital shape: l=0 (s, spherical), l=1 (p, dumbbell), l=2 (d, complex).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. l determines shape. n determines size/energy, ml determines orientation, ms determines spin.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The geometry of NH3 molecule is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: NH3: sp3 hybridised, 3 bond pairs + 1 lone pair → trigonal pyramidal shape. Bond angle ~107°.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. NH3 = trigonal pyramidal (3 bp + 1 lp). BF3 = trigonal planar (3 bp + 0 lp).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following molecules has the highest bond order?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.7
questions.ncert_line: Bond order: N2 = 3 (triple bond), O2 = 2 (double bond), F2 = 1 (single bond), H2 = 1 (single bond).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. N2 has triple bond — bond order 3 (highest). Higher bond order = shorter, stronger bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a spontaneous process at constant T and P, the Gibbs free energy change (ΔG) must be:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.5
questions.ncert_line: Spontaneous process: ΔG < 0. ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. At equilibrium ΔG = 0. Non-spontaneous: ΔG > 0.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Spontaneous: ΔG < 0. This is the criterion for spontaneity at constant T and P.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the reaction N2 + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3, the equilibrium constant Kp is related to Kc by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.4
questions.ncert_line: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn. Δn = 2 − (1+3) = −2. So Kp = Kc(RT)⁻².
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Δn = moles of gaseous products − moles of gaseous reactants = 2−4 = −2. Kp = Kc(RT)⁻².

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Le Chatelier's principle states that when a system at equilibrium is disturbed, it will:

options.A: Always shift to produce more products
options.C: Always shift to produce more reactants

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: Le Chatelier's principle: system shifts to oppose the stress and restore equilibrium.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. System shifts to counteract disturbance. Increase pressure → shift to fewer moles of gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a buffer solution?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.12
questions.ncert_line: Buffer = weak acid + its conjugate base OR weak base + its conjugate acid. NaCl + HCl = strong acid/salt, not a buffer.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. NaCl + HCl is NOT a buffer. It's a strong acid + salt with no buffering action.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Electronegativity generally increases along a period because:

options.B: Nuclear charge increases and atomic radius decreases

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.8
questions.ncert_line: Along a period: nuclear charge increases, atomic radius decreases → stronger pull on bonding electrons → higher electronegativity.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Electronegativity ∝ nuclear charge and ∝ 1/atomic radius. Both factors increase left to right.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is thrown horizontally from a height of 20 m. Time to reach the ground is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: h = ½gt² → 20 = ½ × 10 × t² → t² = 4 → t = 2 s.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. h = ½gt² → t = √(2h/g) = √(40/10) = 2 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body starts from rest and accelerates uniformly at 4 m/s². Distance covered in the 5th second is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.4
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Distance in nth second: s_n = u + a(2n−1)/2 = 0 + 4(9)/2 = 18 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Time period of a simple pendulum is independent of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 14 — Oscillations, Section 14.5
questions.ncert_line: T = 2π√(L/g). Time period depends on L and g only — not on mass or amplitude (for small angles).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. T = 2π√(L/g). Mass of bob does NOT affect time period.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The speed of sound in air increases with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.6
questions.ncert_line: v = √(γP/ρ). Humidity replaces heavier O2/N2 with lighter H2O molecules → lower density → higher speed.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Humid air has lower density (H2O lighter than N2/O2) → speed increases. Speed also increases with temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which electromagnetic radiation has the highest frequency?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: EM spectrum order (increasing frequency): Radio < Microwave < IR < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gamma rays have highest frequency and energy. Radio waves have lowest frequency.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a monohybrid cross between Tt × Tt, the phenotypic ratio is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Tt × Tt gives genotypes TT:Tt:tt = 1:2:1. Since T is dominant, TT and Tt show same phenotype. Phenotypic ratio = 3:1.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Phenotypic ratio in Tt × Tt = 3:1 (dominant:recessive).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of co-dominance?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: ABO blood groups show co-dominance — IA and IB alleles are both expressed in AB blood group.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. ABO blood group = co-dominance. IA and IB are co-dominant (both expressed in IAIB individual).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The enzyme that unwinds the DNA double helix during replication is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Helicase breaks hydrogen bonds between complementary bases to unwind and separate the two strands.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Helicase unwinds DNA. DNA polymerase adds nucleotides. Ligase joins fragments. Primase makes RNA primer.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In E. coli, the lac operon is induced by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.6
questions.ncert_line: Allolactose (a metabolite of lactose) binds the lac repressor and inactivates it, allowing transcription of lac genes.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Allolactose (derived from lactose) is the inducer — inactivates repressor → lac operon transcribed.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Industrial melanism in Biston betularia moth is an example of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.4
questions.ncert_line: Dark moths survived better on soot-covered trees (camouflage) → natural selection favoured melanic forms.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Industrial melanism = natural selection. Melanic moths had survival advantage on dark tree bark.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The concept of 'Punctuated Equilibrium' was proposed by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.5
questions.ncert_line: Punctuated equilibrium (rapid evolution interspersed with long stable periods) was proposed by Gould and Eldredge.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gould and Eldredge proposed punctuated equilibrium (1972).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The vaccine for smallpox was developed by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.1
questions.ncert_line: Edward Jenner developed the smallpox vaccine in 1796 using cowpox virus (vaccinia).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Edward Jenner — smallpox vaccine (1796). First vaccine ever developed.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Heterospory (production of two kinds of spores) is first seen in:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: Heterospory first evolved in pteridophytes (e.g. Selaginella, Salvinia). This is considered a precursor to seed habit.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Heterospory first appears in pteridophytes (Selaginella). Leads to evolution of seed habit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which gymnosperm is called a 'living fossil'?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 3 — Plant Kingdom, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Ginkgo biloba is called a living fossil — it is the only surviving species of the order Ginkgoales.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ginkgo biloba = living fossil. Cycas = common gymnosperm. Pinus = pine tree.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Venation in monocot leaves is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 5 — Morphology of Flowering Plants, Section 5.3
questions.ncert_line: Monocot leaves have parallel venation. Dicot leaves have reticulate (network) venation.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Monocots = parallel venation. Dicots = reticulate venation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a tap root system?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 5 — Morphology of Flowering Plants, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Fibrous and adventitious roots are features of monocots, not tap root systems.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Fibrous and adventitious roots = monocot feature. Tap root = dicot, from radicle.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The tissue responsible for lateral growth (secondary growth) in dicot stems is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 6 — Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Vascular cambium (lateral meristem) produces secondary xylem inward and secondary phloem outward.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Vascular cambium = secondary growth. Produces wood (secondary xylem) and bast (secondary phloem).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In SN2 reactions, the nucleophile attacks from:

options.B: Opposite side to the leaving group (backside attack)

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: SN2 mechanism: nucleophile attacks carbon from opposite side (180°) to leaving group — inversion of configuration.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. SN2 = backside attack → Walden inversion. Complete inversion of stereochemistry.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Hyperconjugation involves delocalisation of electrons from:

options.A: π bond to adjacent p orbital
options.B: σ C-H bond to adjacent empty p orbital or π system
options.C: Lone pair to adjacent π bond

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Hyperconjugation: electrons from C-H σ bond delocalise into adjacent π system or empty p orbital.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Hyperconjugation = σ C-H electrons delocalised into adjacent π or empty p orbital. More C-H bonds = more stability.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Markovnikov's rule states that in addition of HX to an alkene, the H adds to the carbon with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Markovnikov's rule: H adds to carbon with more H atoms (less substituted carbon). X adds to more substituted carbon.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Markovnikov: H goes to carbon with more H (less substituted). This forms more stable carbocation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following undergoes electrophilic substitution rather than addition?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Benzene undergoes electrophilic aromatic substitution (not addition) to preserve aromaticity.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Benzene = electrophilic substitution (preserves aromaticity). Alkenes = electrophilic addition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The IUPAC name of CHCl3 is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: CHCl3 = trichloromethane (IUPAC). Common name = chloroform. CH2Cl2 = dichloromethane.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CHCl3 = trichloromethane. CHCl3 is chloroform (common name). CCl4 = tetrachloromethane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The secondary structure of proteins is maintained by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.4
questions.ncert_line: Secondary structure (alpha helix, beta sheet) is maintained by hydrogen bonds between -C=O and -N-H groups.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. H-bonds stabilise secondary structure (α-helix, β-pleated sheet).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency causes scurvy?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.5
questions.ncert_line: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) deficiency causes scurvy — characterised by bleeding gums and poor wound healing.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Vitamin C deficiency → scurvy. Vitamin D deficiency → rickets. Vitamin A deficiency → night blindness.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nylon-6,6 is formed by condensation polymerisation of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 15 — Polymers, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Nylon-6,6: formed from hexamethylene diamine (6C) + adipic acid (6C) → polyamide with peptide-like linkages.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Nylon-6,6 = hexamethylene diamine + adipic acid. Nylon-6 = caprolactam only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a pure resistor AC circuit, voltage and current are:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.3
questions.ncert_line: In a pure resistor, V and I are always in phase (phase difference = 0).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pure R: V and I in phase. Pure L: I lags V by 90°. Pure C: I leads V by 90°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, the impedance is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: At resonance: XL = XC, so they cancel. Z = √(R² + (XL−XC)²) = √(R²) = R.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. At resonance Z = R (minimum impedance). Current is maximum at resonance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rms value of an AC voltage V = V0 sinωt is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.2
questions.ncert_line: Vrms = V0/√2 = 0.707 V0. For household supply: 230 V rms means peak voltage = 230√2 ≈ 325 V.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Vrms = V0/√2. Similarly Irms = I0/√2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a common emitter transistor, the current gain β is defined as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.8
questions.ncert_line: β (DC current gain) = IC/IB. Typical values: 20-200. β = α/(1−α) where α = IC/IE.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. β = IC/IB. The base current controls larger collector current — transistor action.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which logic gate gives output 1 only when all inputs are 0?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.9
questions.ncert_line: NOR gate: output = 1 only when ALL inputs = 0. It is the complement of OR gate.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. NOR = NOT OR. Output 1 only when both inputs are 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Within the elastic limit, stress is proportional to strain. This is known as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.3
questions.ncert_line: Hooke's law: stress ∝ strain (within elastic limit). Stress/strain = elastic modulus (constant).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Hooke's law: stress ∝ strain. E = stress/strain = Young's modulus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When water at 0°C is heated to 4°C, its density:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.6
questions.ncert_line: Water has anomalous expansion — density increases from 0°C to 4°C, maximum density at 4°C.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Water density increases 0→4°C (anomalous expansion). Maximum density at 4°C = 1000 kg/m³.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to kinetic theory, temperature of a gas is a measure of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Temperature is proportional to average translational kinetic energy per molecule: ½m<v²> = (3/2)kT.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. T ∝ average KE per molecule. KE = (3/2)kT per molecule.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The relationship between clownfish and sea anemone is an example of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Clownfish gets shelter and protection from sea anemone; anemone benefits from waste nutrients and cleaning by clownfish — mutual benefit.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Clownfish + sea anemone = mutualism (+/+). Both benefit from the relationship.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents a sigmoid (logistic) growth curve?

options.B: J-shaped curve with no carrying capacity
options.C: S-shaped curve with upper asymptote at K
options.D: Linear growth followed by sudden decline

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Logistic growth is S-shaped (sigmoid). Population grows slowly, then rapidly, then levels off at carrying capacity K.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Logistic growth = S-shaped curve. Upper asymptote = K (carrying capacity).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a pioneer species in primary succession?

options.B: They modify the environment for future species
options.C: They have high competitive ability in stable environments

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.5
questions.ncert_line: Pioneer species are stress-tolerant but NOT highly competitive — they are replaced by more competitive species as succession proceeds.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pioneers have LOW competitive ability. They are r-strategists — good at colonising, poor at competing.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: BOD (Biochemical Oxygen Demand) is a measure of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: BOD measures the amount of dissolved oxygen needed by microorganisms to decompose organic matter in water.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. High BOD = high organic pollution. Clean water has BOD < 5 ppm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The functional unit of the kidney is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and their Elimination, Section 19.1
questions.ncert_line: The nephron is the structural and functional unit of the kidney. Each kidney has about 1 million nephrons.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Nephron = functional unit. Each kidney has ~1 million nephrons.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of releasing urine from the urinary bladder is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and their Elimination, Section 19.3
questions.ncert_line: Micturition (urination) is the process of releasing stored urine from the urinary bladder through the urethra.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Micturition = urination. Controlled by both voluntary and involuntary mechanisms.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The protein that forms the thin filament in muscle is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 20 — Locomotion and Movement, Section 20.2
questions.ncert_line: Thin filaments are composed of actin (F-actin), troponin and tropomyosin. Myosin forms thick filaments.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Actin = thin filament. Myosin = thick filament. Ca²⁺ binds troponin to expose actin binding sites.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which hormone is called the 'emergency hormone' or 'hormone of fight or flight'?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.3
questions.ncert_line: Adrenaline (epinephrine) secreted by adrenal medulla prepares body for fight or flight — increases heart rate, dilates pupils.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Adrenaline = fight or flight hormone. Secreted by adrenal medulla.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Diabetes insipidus is caused by deficiency of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.2
questions.ncert_line: ADH (antidiuretic hormone) promotes water reabsorption in kidneys. Deficiency → excess dilute urine = diabetes insipidus.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ADH deficiency → diabetes insipidus (not mellitus). Diabetes mellitus = insulin deficiency.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which allotrope of phosphorus is most reactive?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: White phosphorus is the most reactive allotrope — it ignites spontaneously in air at 35°C (P4 molecules with high ring strain).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. White phosphorus most reactive — stored under water. Red phosphorus is stable and less reactive.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxoacids of sulphur contains an S-S bond?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.9
questions.ncert_line: Thiosulphuric acid H2S2O3 has an S-S bond (one S replaces O in sulphuric acid). Pyrosulphuric acid has S-O-S linkage.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. H2S2O3 (thiosulphuric acid) contains S-S bond. H2S2O7 has S-O-S linkage, no S-S bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: The d and f Block Elements
questions.question: Which of the following transition metals is liquid at room temperature?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature (melting point −39°C).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Mercury (Hg) is liquid at room temperature. It is a transition metal (d-block element).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: The d and f Block Elements
questions.question: The colour of transition metal complexes is due to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.5
questions.ncert_line: Transition metals absorb visible light for d-d transitions. The complementary colour is observed.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. d-d transitions absorb specific wavelengths → complementary colour observed. Cu²⁺ = blue, Cr³⁺ = violet.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of iron in [Fe(CN)6]³⁻ is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: Charge of complex = −3. CN⁻ has charge −1 × 6 = −6. Fe + (−6) = −3. Fe = +3.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Fe + 6(−1) = −3 → Fe = +3. [Fe(CN)6]⁴⁻ has Fe²⁺. [Fe(CN)6]³⁻ has Fe³⁺.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Adsorption of gases on solid surfaces is generally:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Adsorption is always exothermic — ΔH is negative. Entropy decreases so ΔS < 0. ΔG = ΔH − TΔS < 0 for spontaneous adsorption.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Adsorption is exothermic (ΔH < 0). As temperature increases, adsorption decreases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reverse osmosis is used in water purification. It involves applying pressure:

options.A: Less than osmotic pressure on pure water side
options.B: Greater than osmotic pressure on solution side
options.C: Equal to osmotic pressure on both sides
options.D: Less than osmotic pressure on solution side

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4
questions.ncert_line: In RO, pressure greater than osmotic pressure is applied on solution side → solvent flows from solution to pure water side.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RO: pressure > π applied on solution side → water forced through semipermeable membrane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following sets of quantum numbers is NOT possible?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Structure of Atom, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: For n=2, l can be 0 or 1 only (l < n). l=2 when n=2 is NOT allowed.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. l must be < n. n=2 allows l=0,1 only. l=2 requires n ≥ 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard enthalpy of formation of an element in its standard state is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: By definition, ΔfH° of any element in its standard state = 0 (reference state).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔfH° = 0 for all elements in standard state. e.g. ΔfH°(O2, g) = 0, ΔfH°(C, graphite) = 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The shape of XeF4 molecule is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Bonding, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: XeF4: sp3d2 hybridisation. 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs (octahedral electron geometry). Lone pairs at opposite axial positions → square planar shape.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. XeF4 = square planar. 2 lone pairs in axial positions cancel out → square planar molecular shape.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Bernoulli's theorem is based on the principle of conservation of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Bernoulli's theorem: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant. It is conservation of energy per unit volume.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Bernoulli's theorem = conservation of energy (pressure + kinetic + potential energy = constant).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A steel needle can float on water due to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.5
questions.ncert_line: A steel needle placed carefully on water floats due to surface tension — water surface acts like a stretched membrane.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Needle floats due to surface tension, not buoyancy (steel is denser than water).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Newton's law of cooling states that rate of heat loss is proportional to:

options.C: Temperature difference between body and surroundings

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.9
questions.ncert_line: Newton's law of cooling: dQ/dt ∝ (T − T_surroundings). Valid for small temperature differences.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. dQ/dt ∝ (T − T0). Valid when temperature difference is small. Stefan's law for large differences.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rms speed of gas molecules is proportional to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: vrms = √(3RT/M) ∝ √T at constant molar mass. Doubling T increases rms speed by √2.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. vrms ∝ √T. At constant M, doubling T increases vrms by factor √2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Electric field lines never intersect each other because:

options.B: At intersection point, field would have two directions which is impossible
options.D: They always go from positive to negative

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.10
questions.ncert_line: If field lines crossed, the field at that point would have two directions simultaneously — which is physically impossible.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Two field lines intersecting would mean two directions of E at one point — impossible.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The SI unit of resistivity is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5
questions.ncert_line: Resistivity ρ = RA/L. Units = Ω × m² / m = Ω·m.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Resistivity unit = Ω·m. Conductivity σ = 1/ρ has unit S/m or Ω⁻¹m⁻¹.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The materials that are strongly attracted by a magnetic field are called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.4
questions.ncert_line: Ferromagnetic materials (iron, nickel, cobalt) are strongly attracted to magnetic fields and can be permanently magnetised.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ferromagnetic = strongly attracted. Paramagnetic = weakly attracted. Diamagnetic = weakly repelled.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a condition for total internal reflection?

options.A: Light must travel from denser to rarer medium
options.B: Angle of incidence must exceed critical angle
options.C: Light must travel from rarer to denser medium

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: TIR occurs when light travels from DENSER to RARER medium and angle of incidence exceeds critical angle.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. TIR needs denser→rarer medium (not rarer→denser). e.g. glass→air, not air→glass.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The powerhouse of the cell is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Mitochondria produce ATP via oxidative phosphorylation — hence called the powerhouse of the cell.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Mitochondria = powerhouse. Site of Krebs cycle and oxidative phosphorylation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The 9+2 arrangement of microtubules is found in:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.5
questions.ncert_line: Cilia and flagella have 9+2 arrangement: 9 peripheral doublet microtubules + 2 central singlet microtubules (axoneme).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cilia/flagella = 9+2 axoneme. Centrioles = 9+0 (triplets, no central tubules).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for modifying, sorting and packaging proteins for secretion?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.6
questions.ncert_line: Golgi apparatus (Golgi complex) is the cell's post office — modifies proteins from ER and packages them for secretion.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Golgi apparatus = modification + packaging + secretion. ER = synthesis of proteins and lipids.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes) occurs during which sub-stage of Prophase I?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Synapsis (formation of synaptonemal complex between homologous chromosomes) occurs during zygotene.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zygotene = synapsis. Leptotene = chromosomes first visible. Pachytene = crossing over.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The DNA content per cell in G2 phase compared to G1 phase is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 10 — Cell Cycle and Cell Division, Section 10.1
questions.ncert_line: S phase (between G1 and G2) replicates DNA. So G2 has double the DNA content of G1.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. G2 DNA = 2 × G1 DNA. S phase replication doubles DNA content.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following growth models shows a J-shaped curve?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Exponential (geometric) growth: dN/dt = rN gives J-shaped curve with no upper limit.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Exponential growth = J-shaped curve. Logistic growth = S-shaped (sigmoidal) curve with K as upper limit.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The interaction where both species are harmed is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Competition (−/−): both species are harmed. Mutualism (+/+), Predation (+/−), Commensalism (+/0).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Competition = both harmed (−/−). Gause's competitive exclusion principle applies here.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The 10% energy transfer law in a food chain was given by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.3
questions.ncert_line: Lindemann (1942) proposed the 10% law — only 10% of energy at one trophic level transfers to the next.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Lindemann's 10% law. Only 10% energy passes to next trophic level; 90% lost as heat.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: N2 is NOT a greenhouse gas. CO2, CH4, N2O, H2O vapour and CFCs are greenhouse gases.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. N2 is not a greenhouse gas. It makes up 78% of atmosphere but doesn't trap heat.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The IUCN Red List categorises a species as 'Extinct in the Wild' when:

options.A: It survives only in captivity or cultivation
options.C: It cannot be located in its habitat
options.D: It has fewer than 50 mature individuals

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 15 — Biodiversity and Conservation, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: IUCN category 'Extinct in the Wild' = species survives only in captivity or as naturalised population outside its range.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Extinct in Wild = survives only in captivity/cultivation. Critically Endangered = <50 mature individuals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Adsorption of gases on solids generally increases with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: Adsorption increases with surface area (more sites available) and decreases with temperature.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. More surface area = more adsorption sites. Finely divided adsorbent > coarse material.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a lyophilic colloid?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 5 — Surface Chemistry, Section 5.4
questions.ncert_line: Lyophilic colloids: dispersed phase has affinity for dispersion medium. Starch, gelatin, gum = lyophilic.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Starch = lyophilic (solvent-loving). Gold sol, AS2S3, sulphur sol = lyophobic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of atoms per unit cell in a face-centred cubic (FCC) structure is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — The Solid State, Section 1.3
questions.ncert_line: FCC: 8 corner atoms (×1/8) + 6 face atoms (×1/2) = 1 + 3 = 4 atoms per unit cell.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. FCC = 4 atoms/unit cell. BCC = 2. Simple cubic = 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Schottky defect in ionic crystals causes:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — The Solid State, Section 1.5
questions.ncert_line: Schottky defect: equal number of cations and anions missing → fewer ions → lower density.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Schottky defect decreases density (missing ions). Frenkel defect: no density change (ions displaced, not missing).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The standard EMF of a cell is related to Gibbs free energy by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.6
questions.ncert_line: ΔG° = −nFE°. Spontaneous cell reaction: E° > 0, so ΔG° < 0 (spontaneous). n = moles of electrons, F = Faraday.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. ΔG° = −nFE°. Also: ΔG° = −RT ln K. So E° = RT ln K / nF.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Bernoulli's equation is based on conservation of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Bernoulli's theorem: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant. It is derived from conservation of energy.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Bernoulli's principle = conservation of energy for fluid flow.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body floats in a liquid when:

options.D: Its volume equals displaced liquid volume

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Archimedes' principle: body floats when buoyant force (weight of displaced liquid) = weight of body.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Float condition: Buoyant force = Weight. F_b = ρ_liquid × V_submerged × g = mg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A concave mirror of focal length 15 cm forms an image at infinity. The object is at:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: For concave mirror: image at infinity when object is at focus (u = f = 15 cm).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Image at infinity → object at focus. u = f = 15 cm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The phenomenon of splitting of white light into its component colours is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.9
questions.ncert_line: Dispersion: splitting of white light into VIBGYOR spectrum. Occurs because different colours have different refractive indices.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Dispersion = splitting of white light. Violet bends most, red bends least (prism).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric flux through a closed surface enclosing a charge q is (Gauss's law):
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.13
questions.ncert_line: Gauss's law: φ = q_enclosed/ε₀. Electric flux through closed surface = total enclosed charge / ε₀.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. φ = q/ε₀ (Gauss's law). Independent of size/shape of surface.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A material that is repelled by a magnetic field is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.3
questions.ncert_line: Diamagnetic materials (e.g. Cu, Bi, water) are weakly repelled by magnetic fields. χm is small and negative.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Diamagnetic = repelled by B field. Paramagnetic = weakly attracted. Ferromagnetic = strongly attracted.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: The work function of a metal is 2 eV. The threshold frequency for photoelectric emission is: (h = 6.6×10⁻³⁴ J·s)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation, Section 11.3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The half-life of a radioactive substance is 20 days. After 60 days, the fraction remaining is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: Number of half-lives = 60/20 = 3. Fraction remaining = (1/2)³ = 1/8.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Absorption of glucose and amino acids in the small intestine occurs by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 16 — Digestion and Absorption, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: Glucose and amino acids are absorbed by active transport using Na+ co-transport across intestinal epithelium.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Active transport with Na+ gradient. Fatty acids absorbed differently — as micelles into lymph.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The tidal volume of a normal adult at rest is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 17 — Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Section 17.2
questions.ncert_line: Tidal volume (TV) = volume of air inspired/expired in one normal breath ≈ 500 mL.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Tidal volume ≈ 500 mL. IRV ≈ 2500 mL. ERV ≈ 1000 mL. RV ≈ 1100 mL.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The blood group that is called 'universal donor' is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 18 — Body Fluids and Circulation, Section 18.2
questions.ncert_line: O− (O negative) is the universal donor — no A, B antigens and no Rh factor, so can donate to any blood group.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. O− = universal donor. AB+ = universal recipient.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Loop of Henle is INCORRECT?

options.A: Descending limb is permeable to water
options.B: Ascending limb is impermeable to water
options.C: It helps in concentration of urine
options.D: It is found in all nephrons equally

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 19 — Excretory Products and their Elimination, Section 19.3
questions.ncert_line: Loop of Henle is long only in juxtamedullary nephrons (deep in medulla). Cortical nephrons have short loops.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Long loop of Henle only in juxtamedullary nephrons, not all nephrons equally.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The protein absent in smooth muscle compared to striated muscle is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 20 — Locomotion and Movement, Section 20.2
questions.ncert_line: Smooth muscle lacks troponin. Striated (skeletal/cardiac) muscle has actin, myosin AND troponin-tropomyosin complex.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Smooth muscle lacks troponin. Ca²⁺ regulation differs — uses calmodulin instead.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The hormone that regulates blood calcium levels is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.4
questions.ncert_line: PTH (parathormone) from parathyroid glands increases blood Ca²⁺ by stimulating bone resorption and kidney reabsorption.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. PTH raises blood Ca²⁺. Calcitonin (thyroid) lowers blood Ca²⁺. They are antagonistic.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which type of epithelium lines the inner surface of blood vessels?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.2
questions.ncert_line: Blood vessels are lined by simple squamous epithelium called endothelium — allows easy diffusion.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Endothelium (simple squamous) lines blood vessels. Allows smooth blood flow.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cartilage is an example of which type of tissue?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.3
questions.ncert_line: Cartilage is a specialised connective tissue with chondrocytes embedded in a matrix of chondroitin sulphate.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cartilage = connective tissue. Chondrocytes in lacunae within matrix.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The respiratory organs of cockroach are:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 7 — Structural Organisation in Animals, Section 7.4
questions.ncert_line: Cockroaches breathe through tracheae (network of tubes) that open via spiracles on the body surface.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cockroach breathes through tracheae (not lungs). Book lungs = spiders. Gills = aquatic organisms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Inductive effect is transmitted through:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Inductive effect is transmitted through σ bonds and decreases rapidly with distance from the functional group.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Inductive effect through σ bonds, decreases with distance. Resonance through π bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The stability order of carbocations is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Carbocation stability: 3° > 2° > 1° > methyl. More alkyl groups = more hyperconjugation and +I effect = more stable.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 3° most stable carbocation. More alkyl groups provide electron density via hyperconjugation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The combustion of alkanes is an example of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Hydrocarbons, Section 13.2
questions.ncert_line: Complete combustion: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O. Oxidation releases energy (exothermic).
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Combustion = oxidation. Alkanes also undergo halogenation (free radical substitution).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which reagent is used to convert alkyl halide to alkane (reduction)?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn/HCl or LiAlH4 reduces R-X to R-H (alkane). Alcoholic KOH causes dehydrohalogenation (elimination).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zn/HCl or LiAlH4 reduces R-X → R-H. Alcoholic KOH → alkene (elimination).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following reactions is used to distinguish primary, secondary and tertiary amines?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Hinsberg test: benzenesulphonyl chloride reacts differently with 1°, 2°, 3° amines — used to distinguish them.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Hinsberg test distinguishes 1°, 2°, 3° amines. Lucas test distinguishes 1°, 2°, 3° alcohols.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aniline reacts with acetic anhydride to give:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Aniline + (CH3CO)2O → CH3CONHC6H5 (acetanilide) + CH3COOH. Acetylation of amine.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Aniline + acetic anhydride → acetanilide. This is acetylation (protection of amino group).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The area under a velocity-time graph gives:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Area under v-t graph = ∫v dt = displacement. Slope of v-t graph = acceleration.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Area under v-t graph = displacement. Area under a-t graph = change in velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A projectile is launched at 60° to horizontal with speed 20 m/s. Maximum height reached is: (g=10 m/s²)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The gravitational potential energy of a body of mass m at height h above Earth surface is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: For h << R (Earth radius), gravitational PE = mgh (taking surface as reference). Exact formula: −GMm/(R+h).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. GPE = mgh for small heights. Negative sign in exact formula indicates bound state.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A cell of EMF 2V and internal resistance 0.5Ω is connected to an external resistance of 1.5Ω. Current in circuit is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The SI unit of magnetic field (B) is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: SI unit of B (magnetic flux density) is Tesla (T). 1 T = 1 Wb/m² = 1 kg/(A·s²). CGS unit = Gauss (1T = 10⁴ G).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Tesla is SI unit of B. Weber = unit of magnetic flux. 1 T = 1 Wb/m².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Power factor of a purely inductive AC circuit is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.5
questions.ncert_line: Power factor = cosφ. In pure inductor φ = 90°, so cosφ = cos90° = 0. No real power consumed.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pure inductor: φ = 90°, power factor = cos90° = 0. No power dissipated.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): DNA replication is semi-conservative. Reason (R): Each new DNA molecule contains one old and one new strand.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Semi-conservative replication (proved by Meselson-Stahl): each daughter DNA has one parental + one new strand. R correctly explains A.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. Meselson-Stahl experiment (1958) confirmed semi-conservative replication.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): C4 plants are more efficient in hot, sunny climates. Reason (R): C4 plants avoid photorespiration by concentrating CO2 in bundle sheath cells.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: C4 plants (maize, sugarcane) use PEP carboxylase in mesophyll to fix CO2 into C4 acids, then release CO2 in bundle sheath — bypassing photorespiration.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. C4 plants have CO2-concentrating mechanism eliminating photorespiration.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Myelinated nerve fibres conduct impulses faster than unmyelinated fibres. Reason (R): In myelinated fibres, impulse jumps from one node of Ranvier to the next (saltatory conduction).

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 21 — Neural Control and Coordination, Section 21.2
questions.ncert_line: Saltatory conduction in myelinated fibres: impulse jumps node to node (skipping myelin sheath) — much faster than continuous conduction.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R correctly explains A. Saltatory conduction = faster impulse in myelinated fibres.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Wind-pollinated flowers are generally small and inconspicuous. Reason (R): Wind-pollinated flowers do not need to attract pollinators.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.3
questions.ncert_line: Anemophilous (wind-pollinated) flowers: small, light pollen, no nectar/fragrance — no need to attract pollinators.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true and R explains A. Wind pollination: light pollen, feathery stigma, no colourful petals.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Assertion (A): Decomposers are essential for nutrient cycling in an ecosystem. Reason (R): Decomposers convert complex organic matter into inorganic nutrients available to producers.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.4
questions.ncert_line: Decomposers (bacteria, fungi) mineralise organic matter → release inorganic nutrients → used by producers. Essential for nutrient cycling.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R explains A. Without decomposers, nutrients would remain locked in dead organic matter.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a dihybrid cross (AaBb × AaBb), the phenotypic ratio is 9:3:3:1. The number of plants showing both recessive traits is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: 9:3:3:1 ratio. The '1' represents aabb (both recessive) = 1/16 of offspring.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. aabb = 1 out of 16. Students often confuse with the '3' groups.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Viruses are NOT included in any kingdom of the five-kingdom classification because:

options.B: They are acellular and can replicate only inside a host cell
options.D: They have no metabolism of their own when outside a host

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.5
questions.ncert_line: Viruses are acellular (no cell structure) and are obligate intracellular parasites — they don't fit any kingdom.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Viruses = acellular, obligate intracellular. Neither living nor non-living — they are placed outside five kingdoms.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a macronutrient required by plants?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Macronutrients: C, H, O, N, P, K, Ca, Mg, S. Iron, Mn, Zn are micronutrients. Mg is a macronutrient (in chlorophyll).
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Magnesium is a macronutrient (part of chlorophyll). Fe, Mn, Zn are micronutrients. Common exam trap.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The enzyme that is called a 'biological catalyst' works by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Enzymes lower the activation energy barrier — more substrate molecules can overcome it, speeding up the reaction.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Enzymes LOWER activation energy. They do NOT change equilibrium or provide energy.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Insulin is produced by which cells of the pancreas?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 22 — Chemical Coordination and Integration, Section 22.5
questions.ncert_line: Beta cells of islets of Langerhans produce insulin. Alpha cells produce glucagon. Delta cells produce somatostatin.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Beta cells → insulin. Alpha cells → glucagon. Common confusion in exams.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Assertion (A): Conductivity of electrolytic solutions decreases on dilution. Reason (R): On dilution, ions move farther apart reducing inter-ionic attraction.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is false, R is true
options.D: A is false, R is false

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: On dilution: conductivity (κ) DECREASES (fewer ions/volume) but molar conductivity (Λm) INCREASES. So assertion A is FALSE.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. A is false — conductivity decreases on dilution but molar conductivity increases. R is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Zero-order: t1/2 = [A]₀/2k. Half-life is directly proportional to initial concentration. (Opposite of first order.)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zero order t1/2 = [A]₀/2k ∝ [A]₀. First order t1/2 = 0.693/k (independent of concentration).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Elevation in boiling point is a colligative property because it depends on:

options.C: Number of solute particles in solution

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4
questions.ncert_line: Colligative properties depend only on the NUMBER of solute particles (moles), not on the nature or identity of solute.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Colligative = depends on number of particles only, not nature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes has zero dipole moment?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: trans-[PtCl2(NH3)2]: identical ligands opposite each other → dipole moments cancel → zero net dipole moment.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. trans isomer: Cl-Pt-Cl and NH3-Pt-NH3 are linear — dipoles cancel. cis isomer has net dipole.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest oxidising agent among halogens?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.13
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of halogens: F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2. F2 is strongest oxidising agent (highest E° = +2.87 V).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F2 strongest oxidising agent. Oxidising power decreases down the group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metal ions is colourless?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn²+ has d¹⁰ configuration — completely filled d orbitals. No d-d transition possible → colourless.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn²+ (d¹⁰) is colourless. Colour in transition metal ions requires incomplete d subshell.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The first ionisation energy of Al is less than that of Mg because:

options.B: Al's 3p electron is easier to remove than Mg's 3s electron

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.7
questions.ncert_line: Al [Ne]3s²3p¹: the 3p electron has higher energy and is better shielded than Mg's 3s² electrons → easier to remove.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Al (3p¹) < Mg (3s²) ionisation energy. 3p electron more easily removed than 3s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 10 kg block rests on a frictionless surface. A 100 N force is applied. The acceleration is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two charges +q and −q are placed at distance d apart. The electric field at the midpoint between them is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.7
questions.ncert_line: At midpoint: E from +q points away (toward −q) = kq/(d/2)² = 4kq/d². E from −q also points toward −q. Both fields ADD → 8kq/d² total. Wait: total = 4kq/d² + 4kq/d² = 8kq/d²... Let me give cleaner option.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Both fields point in same direction (from +q toward −q) and add up.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Diffraction of light is more pronounced with obstacles comparable in size to wavelength of light. Reason (R): Diffraction is a wave phenomenon depending on the ratio of obstacle size to wavelength.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Diffraction is maximum when obstacle/slit size ≈ wavelength. R correctly explains A using wave physics.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R explains A. Diffraction most pronounced when d ≈ λ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In hydrogen spectrum, the series that lies in the visible region is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Balmer series (n=2 transitions): visible light (400-700 nm). Lyman = UV. Paschen, Brackett, Pfund = infrared.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Balmer series = visible. Lyman = UV (n→1). Paschen = IR (n→3). Common exam confusion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a p-type semiconductor, the majority carriers are:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.4
questions.ncert_line: p-type semiconductor: doped with trivalent impurity (e.g. B, Al). Holes are majority carriers; electrons are minority.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. p-type: holes are majority carriers. n-type: electrons are majority carriers.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The plant hormone that promotes cell elongation and is responsible for phototropism is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) promotes cell elongation. In phototropism, auxin migrates to the shaded side causing unequal growth.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin causes phototropism. Unequal distribution → shaded side elongates → bending toward light.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bolting (elongation of internodes before flowering) is promoted by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.4
questions.ncert_line: Gibberellins promote bolting (rapid stem elongation) in rosette plants like cabbage before flowering.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gibberellin → bolting. Also promotes seed germination and fruit development.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone is called the 'stress hormone' because it promotes seed dormancy and stomatal closure?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: ABA = abscisic acid is the stress hormone. Promotes stomatal closure during water stress, inhibits growth.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. ABA = stress hormone. Closes stomata, promotes dormancy, inhibits seed germination.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The mechanism of opening of stomata is due to:

options.A: Decrease in osmotic pressure of guard cells
options.B: Increase in turgor pressure of guard cells
options.C: Decrease in turgor pressure of guard cells
options.D: Loss of water from guard cells

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 11 — Transport in Plants, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Stomata open when guard cells accumulate K+ ions → water enters by osmosis → turgor increases → stomata open.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Turgor pressure increases in guard cells → stomata open. K+ uptake drives water in.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nitrogen fixation by Rhizobium occurs in root nodules in the presence of enzyme:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Nitrogenase enzyme complex (in Rhizobium) catalyses: N2 + 8H+ + 8e- → 2NH3 + H2. Requires anaerobic conditions.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Nitrogenase fixes N2 → NH3 in root nodules. Leghaemoglobin maintains low O2 to protect nitrogenase.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The number of ATP molecules produced by complete oxidation of one glucose molecule (via aerobic respiration) is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 14 — Respiration in Plants, Section 14.3
questions.ncert_line: Aerobic respiration: ~38 ATP (glycolysis: 2, Krebs: 2, oxidative phosphorylation: 34) from one glucose molecule.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ~38 ATP from aerobic respiration. 2 from glycolysis, 2 from Krebs cycle, 34 from electron transport chain.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Calvin cycle (dark reactions) occurs in which part of the chloroplast?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Calvin cycle (dark reactions/C3 pathway) occurs in the stroma of chloroplast. Light reactions occur in thylakoids.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Calvin cycle = stroma. Light reactions = thylakoid membrane. ATP+NADPH from light reactions used in stroma.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Nematocysts (stinging cells) are found in which phylum?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Cnidarians (coelenterates) have cnidoblasts/cnidocytes containing nematocysts used for prey capture and defence.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Nematocysts in Cnidaria (Hydra, jellyfish, sea anemone). Sponges have spicules/spongin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which is the correct sequence of larval stages in frog development?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Frog development: zygote → morula (cleavage) → blastula → gastrula → neurula → tadpole → metamorphosis → adult.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Standard sequence: zygote → morula → blastula → gastrula → tadpole.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Flame cells are the excretory organs in:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Platyhelminthes (flatworms) excrete using flame cells (protonephridia). Annelids use nephridia. Insects use Malpighian tubules.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Flame cells = excretory organs of Platyhelminthes. Nephridia = annelids. Malpighian tubules = insects.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which alkali metal reacts most vigorously with water?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — s-Block Elements, Section 10.2
questions.ncert_line: Reactivity of alkali metals with water increases down the group: Li < Na < K < Rb < Cs. Caesium reacts explosively.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Cs reacts most vigorously. Reactivity increases down Group 1 (decreasing ionisation energy).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The anomalous behaviour of lithium compared to other alkali metals is due to:

options.B: Its small size and high charge density (diagonal relationship with Mg)

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — s-Block Elements, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Li has smallest ionic radius and highest charge density among alkali metals → diagonal relationship with Mg.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Li resembles Mg (diagonal relationship) due to similar charge/size ratio.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Formic acid (HCOOH) is stronger than acetic acid (CH3COOH) because:

options.A: Formic acid has lower molecular weight
options.B: CH3 group is electron-donating (+I effect) destabilising formate's conjugate base
options.C: Formic acid has more acidic H atoms
options.D: Formic acid is more soluble in water

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: CH3 group in acetic acid has +I effect → increases electron density → destabilises conjugate base (CH3COO-) → weaker acid than HCOOH.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CH3 (+I effect) destabilises acetate ion → acetic acid weaker. HCOOH pKa=3.74; CH3COOH pKa=4.76.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is formed by the reaction of salicylic acid with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.6
questions.ncert_line: Aspirin synthesis: salicylic acid + acetic anhydride (or acetyl chloride) → acetylsalicylic acid + acetic acid.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Aspirin = acetylation of salicylic acid using acetic anhydride. Industrial process.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation of phenol with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 15 — Polymers, Section 15.4
questions.ncert_line: Bakelite = phenol-formaldehyde resin. Cross-linked thermosetting polymer. First synthetic plastic.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Bakelite = phenol + formaldehyde. Cross-linked, thermosetting, does not remelt.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A reaction has ΔH = −100 kJ and ΔS = −200 J/K. At what temperature will the reaction become non-spontaneous?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.5
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Non-spontaneous above 500 K. At T = 500K: ΔG = 0 (equilibrium). Above 500K: ΔG > 0 (non-spontaneous).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following salts gives an alkaline solution when dissolved in water?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.12
questions.ncert_line: CH3COONa = salt of strong base (NaOH) + weak acid (CH3COOH). CH3COO- hydrolyses → CH3COOH + OH- → alkaline.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Sodium acetate → alkaline solution. Salt of weak acid + strong base always gives alkaline solution.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On doubling the distance between two point charges, the force between them becomes:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.6
questions.ncert_line: Coulomb's law: F = kq1q2/r². If r doubles: F' = kq1q2/(2r)² = F/4.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. F ∝ 1/r². Doubling r → force becomes 1/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of 100 turns has flux change of 0.05 Wb in 0.1 s. The induced EMF is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.4
questions.explanation: Correct: C. EMF = N × ΔΦ/Δt = 100 × 0.5 = 50 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: First Bohr radius a₀ = 0.529 Å = 0.0529 nm = 52.9 pm.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. a₀ = 0.529 Å (angstroms). Note: 1 Å = 10⁻¹⁰ m = 0.1 nm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire of length L and cross-sectional area A has Young's modulus Y. Its spring constant is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.3
questions.ncert_line: Spring constant k = Force/extension = (YA/L). From Y = (F/A)/(ΔL/L) → F = YAΔl/L → k = YA/L.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. k = YA/L. More area or shorter wire = stiffer spring.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The root mean square (rms) speed of gas molecules is proportional to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: v_rms = √(3RT/M) ∝ √T. Doubling absolute temperature increases rms speed by √2.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. v_rms ∝ √T. Also v_rms = √(3kT/m) per molecule.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is used in radar systems?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Microwaves (wavelength cm-range) are used in radar (Radio Detection And Ranging) and microwave ovens.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Microwaves in radar. Infrared = heat sensors/remote controls. UV = sterilisation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about the genetic code?

options.B: It is degenerate (multiple codons for one amino acid)
options.D: It is non-ambiguous (one codon = one amino acid)

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5
questions.ncert_line: The genetic code is non-overlapping — each nucleotide belongs to only one codon. It was thought to be overlapping but is not.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Genetic code is NON-overlapping. It IS universal, degenerate, and non-ambiguous.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The template strand of DNA is also called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Template strand = antisense strand = non-coding strand. mRNA sequence matches coding (sense) strand.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Template strand = antisense = non-coding strand. mRNA is complementary to it.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The edible part of coconut that we drink (coconut water) represents:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Coconut water = liquid nuclear endosperm. Coconut flesh = solid cellular endosperm.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Coconut water = liquid endosperm. Coconut flesh = solid endosperm (both are endosperm).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Spermatogenesis occurs in:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.1
