questions.ncert_line: Spermatogenesis (formation of sperms) occurs in seminiferous tubules of the testes.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Seminiferous tubules = site of spermatogenesis. Epididymis = maturation and storage.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which contraceptive method has the HIGHEST failure rate?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Rhythm method (avoiding sex during fertile period) has highest failure rate ~20-25% due to irregular cycles.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Rhythm/calendar method = least reliable. OCP and IUD are most reliable among common methods.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone is responsible for apical dominance?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) produced at apical bud suppresses growth of lateral buds = apical dominance.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin causes apical dominance. Removing apex (decapitation) allows lateral bud growth.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Bolting (premature elongation of stem before flowering) in rosette plants is caused by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Gibberellins cause bolting — rapid internode elongation in rosette plants like cabbage under long days.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Gibberellins cause bolting. Also involved in breaking seed dormancy and fruit development.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The deficiency of which element causes 'little leaf disease' in plants?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: Zinc deficiency causes little leaf disease (small, distorted leaves) and khaira disease in rice.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Zinc deficiency → little leaf disease. Iron deficiency → interveinal chlorosis.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction at 300K: ΔH = −57.1 kJ/mol and ΔS = −80 J/mol/K. The reaction is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.5
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔG = −57100 + 24000 = −33100 J < 0 → spontaneous at 300K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: At a given temperature, Kc for A ⇌ B is 4. If [A] = 2 M at equilibrium, [B] is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.3

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The number of moles in 44 g of CO2 is: (Molecular weight CO2 = 44 g/mol)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry, Section 1.5
questions.ncert_line: Moles = mass/molecular weight = 44/44 = 1 mol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the reaction 2H2 + O2 → 2H2O, the mass of water produced from 4g of H2 is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 1 — Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry, Section 1.6
questions.ncert_line: 4g H2 = 2 mol H2. From equation: 2 mol H2 → 2 mol H2O. 2 mol H2O = 2 × 18 = 36 g.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 4g H2 = 2 mol. 2 mol H2 → 2 mol H2O = 36 g.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a first order reaction, a plot of log[A] vs time gives a straight line with slope:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.3
questions.ncert_line: Integrated first order: log[A] = log[A]₀ − kt/2.303. Slope = −k/2.303.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Slope of log[A] vs t = −k/2.303 for first order reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The mass deposited at cathode when 2 Faradays of charge is passed through CuSO4 solution is: (Cu = 64 g/mol)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu. 2 Faradays = 2 mol e⁻. From equation: 2 mol e⁻ deposits 1 mol Cu = 64 g.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cu²⁺ needs 2F to deposit 1 mol (64g). 2F → 64g Cu.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 2 kg on a frictionless surface is connected to a spring (k = 200 N/m). If compressed 10 cm and released, maximum speed of block is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.4
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Energy conservation: ½mv² = ½kx². v = x√(k/m) = 0.1√100 = 1 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the distance between two masses is tripled, the gravitational force between them becomes:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: F ∝ 1/r². If r → 3r, F → F/9. Force becomes 1/9th.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. F ∝ 1/r². Tripling distance → F becomes 1/9.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor has plate area A and separation d. If a dielectric (κ) is inserted, capacitance becomes:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: With dielectric: C = κε₀A/d. Dielectric constant κ > 1, so capacitance increases.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. C = κε₀A/d. Dielectric increases capacitance by factor κ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of 100 turns has flux change of 0.02 Wb in 0.1 s. The induced EMF is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.3
questions.explanation: Correct: D. EMF = N × ΔΦ/Δt = 100 × 0.02/0.1 = 20 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a single slit diffraction pattern, the width of central maximum is 2λD/a. If slit width a is halved:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Width ∝ 1/a. Halving a doubles the central maximum width.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Width = 2λD/a ∝ 1/a. Halving a → width doubles. Narrower slit → broader diffraction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In hydrogen atom, the wavelength of emitted radiation when electron jumps from n=3 to n=2 is (Hα line):
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Hα line (3→2 transition, Balmer series) has wavelength 656.3 nm — red visible light.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Hα = 656 nm (red). Hβ = 486 nm (blue-green). Both in visible Balmer series.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A p-n junction diode is forward biased when:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.6
questions.ncert_line: Forward bias: p-side to + terminal, n-side to − terminal. This reduces depletion region and allows current.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Forward bias: p→+, n→−. Reduces barrier → current flows. Reverse bias: p→−, n→+ → no current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hydraulic lift has pistons of cross-sectional area 10 cm² and 500 cm². Force applied on small piston is 20 N. Load lifted is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.2
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F2 = 20 × (500/10) = 1000 N. Hydraulic machines multiply force.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Mycorrhiza is a symbiotic association between fungi and:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.2
questions.ncert_line: Mycorrhiza = mutualistic association between fungi and roots of higher plants. Fungi help in mineral absorption.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Mycorrhiza = fungi + plant roots. Lichen = fungi + algae (or cyanobacteria).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: TMV (Tobacco Mosaic Virus) was first crystallised by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: W.M. Stanley crystallised TMV and showed viruses are nucleoproteins. Ivanowsky first discovered virus filterability.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. W.M. Stanley crystallised TMV (1935). Ivanowsky showed infectious agent passes through filter (1892).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of viruses?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 2 — Biological Classification, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Viruses have NO independent metabolism — they are inert outside a host cell and cannot synthesise ATP.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Viruses have no metabolism outside host. They are essentially nucleoprotein packages.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Flame cells are the excretory structures in:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.1
questions.ncert_line: Flame cells (protonephridia) are excretory structures in flatworms (Platyhelminthes) like Planaria.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Flame cells = Platyhelminthes. Nephridia = Annelids. Malpighian tubules = Insects.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The larva of sea squirt (Urochordata) has a notochord but the adult does not. This is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 4 — Animal Kingdom, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Retrogressive metamorphosis: larva is more complex (has notochord) than the degenerate sessile adult.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Retrogressive metamorphosis in Urochordata — larva has notochord, adult loses it.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The main driving force for water movement up the xylem is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Transport in Plants, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Transpiration pull: evaporation from leaves creates tension that pulls water column up through xylem (cohesion-tension theory).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cohesion-tension theory — transpiration pull is the main force for ascent of sap.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which mineral element is needed for nitrogen fixation in Rhizobium?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 12 — Mineral Nutrition, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Molybdenum is a component of nitrogenase enzyme (Mo-Fe protein) essential for biological nitrogen fixation.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Molybdenum is part of nitrogenase. Also needed: Fe (in Fe-protein component of nitrogenase).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Z-scheme of photosynthesis involves:

options.C: Both PS I and PS II in sequence

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Z-scheme: light energy absorbed by PS II → electrons flow to PS I → NADPH produced. Both systems work in series.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Z-scheme involves PS II → electron transport chain → PS I. Generates ATP and NADPH.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Abscisic acid (ABA) is called the 'stress hormone' because it:

options.B: Causes stomatal closure and seed dormancy during stress

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: ABA causes stomatal closure (conserves water) and promotes seed dormancy. It counteracts gibberellins.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. ABA = stress hormone. Closes stomata in drought, promotes dormancy. Antagonises GA.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Vernalisation refers to:

options.B: Requirement of low temperature for flowering

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.6
questions.ncert_line: Vernalisation: low temperature treatment required by certain plants (e.g. wheat) before they can flower.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Vernalisation = cold treatment needed for flowering. Example: winter wheat needs cold period.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest oxidising halogen?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.11
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of halogens: F2 > Cl2 > Br2 > I2. F2 is the strongest oxidising agent among all elements.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. F2 is the strongest oxidising agent. It can oxidise even water: 2F2 + 2H2O → 4HF + O2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following oxoacids of chlorine has the highest oxidising power?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — The p-Block Elements, Section 7.11
questions.ncert_line: Oxidising power of oxoacids decreases as the number of oxygen atoms increases. HClO (Cl in +1) is strongest oxidiser.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. HClO has highest oxidising power among oxoacids of Cl. HClO4 is the strongest acid but weakest oxidiser.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the highest first ionisation energy?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.7
questions.ncert_line: IE generally increases across period, but Mg has higher IE1 than Al. Mg (full 3s²) is more stable than Al (3s²3p¹).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Mg > Al (exception). Full s² subshell in Mg is extra stable. Similarly N > O in same period.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The colour of transition metal compounds is due to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Partially filled d orbitals allow d-d electronic transitions. Energy difference corresponds to visible light → coloured compounds.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. d-d transitions absorb specific visible wavelengths → complementary colour observed. Cu²⁺ = blue (absorbs red).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is classified as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 16 — Chemistry in Everyday Life, Section 16.1
questions.ncert_line: Aspirin is an analgesic (pain-killer), antipyretic (fever-reducer) and anti-inflammatory drug.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Aspirin = analgesic + antipyretic. Also has anticoagulant properties (thins blood).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In nuclear fission of U-235, the energy released per fission is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.7
questions.ncert_line: Each fission of U-235 releases ~200 MeV of energy. This is ~10⁷ times more energy per reaction than chemical combustion.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. U-235 fission ≈ 200 MeV/fission. Chemical reactions: ~few eV. Hence nuclear energy is millions of times more.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which particles are emitted in the decay: ²³⁸U → ²⁰⁶Pb?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.6
questions.ncert_line: Mass change: 238−206=32 → 8α particles (each reduces mass by 4). Atomic number: 92−82=10, but 8α reduces Z by 16, so 6β restores 6. Net: 8α+6β⁻.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. 8 alpha + 6 beta decays: ΔA = 32 (8×4), ΔZ = 10 (−16+6 = −10 gives Pb at Z=82).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The process of superimposing a low-frequency signal on a high-frequency carrier wave is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 15 — Communication Systems, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Modulation: low-frequency message signal is superimposed on high-frequency carrier wave for transmission.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Modulation = message signal + carrier wave. Demodulation = extracting message at receiver.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Sky waves are used for long-distance communication because they are:

options.B: Reflected by the ionosphere back to Earth

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 15 — Communication Systems, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: Sky waves (shortwave, 3-30 MHz) are reflected by the ionosphere back to Earth, enabling long-distance communication.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Sky waves reflected by ionosphere. This allows radio signals to travel beyond horizon.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A liquid drop is spherical due to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.6
questions.ncert_line: Surface tension minimises surface area for a given volume. Sphere has minimum surface area → liquid drops are spherical.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Surface tension → minimum surface area → spherical shape for liquid drops.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Stefan's law states that the power radiated by a black body is proportional to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.9
questions.ncert_line: Stefan-Boltzmann law: P = σAT⁴. Power radiated ∝ T⁴. σ = 5.67×10⁻⁸ W m⁻² K⁻⁴.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. P ∝ T⁴ (Stefan's law). Doubling temperature increases radiated power by 16 times.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: In a dihybrid cross between AaBb × AaBb, the phenotypic ratio is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.1
questions.ncert_line: Standard dihybrid cross AaBb × AaBb gives phenotypic ratio 9:3:3:1 (9 A_B_ : 3 A_bb : 3 aaB_ : 1 aabb).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Dihybrid ratio = 9:3:3:1 when genes are on different chromosomes (independent assortment).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: A man with blood group A (IAi) marries a woman with blood group B (IBi). What fraction of their children can have blood group O?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 5 — Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: IAi × IBi: offspring genotypes IAIB (AB), IAi (A), IBi (B), ii (O) in ratio 1:1:1:1. So 1/4 chance of O.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 1/4 children will be ii (blood group O). ABO = multiple allele inheritance.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Chargaff's rule states that in DNA:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.2
questions.ncert_line: Chargaff's rule: A pairs with T (A = T) and G pairs with C (G = C). Also A+G = T+C (purines = pyrimidines).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. A = T and G = C (complementary base pairing). This confirmed Watson-Crick double helix model.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The process of synthesis of RNA from DNA template is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Transcription: DNA → RNA (using RNA polymerase). Translation: RNA → Protein. Replication: DNA → DNA.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Transcription = DNA → RNA. Translation = RNA → Protein. Both together = gene expression.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which enzyme adds a poly-A tail to eukaryotic mRNA during post-transcriptional modification?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 6 — Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Section 6.5
questions.ncert_line: Poly-A polymerase adds ~200 adenine residues to the 3' end of pre-mRNA — this poly-A tail increases mRNA stability.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Poly-A polymerase adds poly-A tail at 3' end. 5' cap (7-methylguanosine) is added at 5' end.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium is disturbed by all EXCEPT:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.3
questions.ncert_line: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium requires random mating. It is disrupted by selection, genetic drift, mutation, migration, non-random mating.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Random mating is a CONDITION for H-W equilibrium (not a disruptive force).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following provides the strongest evidence for evolution?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 7 — Evolution, Section 7.5
questions.ncert_line: Homologous organs (same fundamental structure, different functions) provide strongest evidence for common ancestry and divergent evolution.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Homologous organs = strongest evidence (divergent evolution from common ancestor). Analogous = convergent evolution.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The acrosome reaction in sperm involves:

options.B: Release of hydrolytic enzymes to penetrate zona pellucida

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Acrosome reaction: acrosome (lysosome-like cap on sperm) releases hydrolytic enzymes to digest zona pellucida.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Acrosome reaction = enzyme release to penetrate zona pellucida. Occurs before fertilisation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Cu-T (copper-T) IUD acts as a contraceptive primarily because copper ions:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 4 — Reproductive Health, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: Copper ions are spermicidal and IUD creates hostile environment preventing implantation of fertilised egg.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Cu-T: copper ions are spermicidal + prevent implantation. Makes uterus hostile.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For a reaction at 298 K, ΔH = −100 kJ/mol and ΔS = −200 J/mol·K. The reaction is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.5
questions.ncert_line: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS = −100000 − T(−200) = −100000 + 200T. ΔG < 0 when T < 500 K. Spontaneous at low T.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ΔH < 0, ΔS < 0 → ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. Spontaneous (ΔG < 0) only at low T (below 500 K).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: At equilibrium, for the reaction N2 + O2 ⇌ 2NO, if Kc = 4 × 10⁻³¹ at 298 K, this means:

options.B: Reaction is reactant-favoured (very little NO at equilibrium)
options.C: Equal amounts of reactants and products

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.4
questions.ncert_line: Very small Kc (4×10⁻³¹) means equilibrium lies far to the left — very little NO at equilibrium. Reaction barely proceeds.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Kc << 1 → reactant-favoured. Almost no NO formed at room temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The elevation in boiling point of 100 g of water when 18 g of glucose (MW=180) is dissolved is: (Kb=0.52 K kg/mol)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4
questions.ncert_line: moles glucose = 18/180 = 0.1 mol. Molality = 0.1/0.1 = 1 mol/kg. ΔTb = Kb × m = 0.52 × 1 = 0.52 K.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. ΔTb = Kb × m = 0.52 × 1.0 = 0.52 K.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The mass of copper deposited when 2 Faradays of electricity is passed through CuSO4 solution is: (Cu=64 g/mol)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Electrochemistry, Section 3.7
questions.ncert_line: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu. 2 Faradays = 2 moles of electrons → 1 mole Cu = 64 g deposited.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Cu²⁺ requires 2e⁻. 2F deposits 1 mol = 64 g Cu.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: A first-order reaction is 75% complete in 32 minutes. Its half-life is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 4 — Chemical Kinetics, Section 4.3
questions.ncert_line: 75% complete means two half-lives (50% after 1st, 75% after 2nd). 2 half-lives = 32 min → t½ = 16 min.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. 75% done = 2 half-lives. 32 min ÷ 2 = 16 min half-life.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following has the smallest atomic radius?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 3 — Classification of Elements, Section 3.6
questions.ncert_line: Atomic radius decreases across a period (increasing nuclear charge). Cl (period 3, group 17) has smallest radius among these.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Cl has smallest radius. Order: Na > Mg > Al > Si > P > S > Cl (left to right).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electric dipole in a uniform electric field experiences:

options.A: A net force and a torque

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.12
questions.ncert_line: In uniform E field: equal and opposite forces on +q and −q cancel → net force = 0. But torque τ = pE sinθ ≠ 0.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Uniform field: no net force, but torque acts to align dipole with field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistance of a conductor doubles if its length is doubled and area is halved. The new resistance is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: R = ρl/A. New R = ρ(2l)/(A/2) = 4ρl/A = 4R.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. R ∝ l/A. Length doubled AND area halved → R becomes 2×2 = 4 times original.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The wavelength range of visible light is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Visible light: 400 nm (violet) to 700 nm (red). UV < 400 nm. IR > 700 nm.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Visible spectrum: 400–700 nm. VIBGYOR: Violet (400nm) → Red (700nm).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a single slit diffraction pattern, the width of the central maximum is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Central maximum width = 2λD/a. Inversely proportional to slit width 'a'. Narrower slit → wider central maximum.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Width ∝ 1/a. Narrower slit → more diffraction → wider central maximum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². The angular acceleration is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — System of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6
questions.ncert_line: τ = Iα → α = τ/I = 10/2 = 5 rad/s².
questions.explanation: Correct: B. α = τ/I = 10/2 = 5 rad/s². (Rotational analogue of F=ma.)

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The bond that links two amino acids in a protein is called:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Peptide bond (-CO-NH-) forms between carboxyl group of one amino acid and amino group of next, with loss of water.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Peptide bond = -CO-NH- between amino acids. Formed by condensation (dehydration).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a competitive inhibitor of an enzyme?

options.A: It permanently destroys the active site
options.B: It binds to allosteric site and changes active site shape
options.C: It resembles the substrate and binds reversibly to active site

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.5
questions.ncert_line: Competitive inhibitor resembles substrate, binds reversibly to active site. Effect reversed by increasing substrate concentration.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Competitive inhibitor competes with substrate for active site. Overcome by excess substrate.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of lipids?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: Genetic information is stored in nucleic acids (DNA/RNA), not lipids. Lipids function in energy storage, membranes and hormones.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Genetic information = nucleic acids. Lipids: energy, membrane structure, steroid hormones, insulation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Km value of an enzyme represents:

options.B: Substrate concentration at half maximum velocity
options.D: pH at which enzyme is most active

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.5
questions.ncert_line: Km (Michaelis constant) = [S] at which reaction rate = Vmax/2. Low Km = high affinity for substrate.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Km = [S] giving half-maximal velocity. Low Km → high enzyme-substrate affinity.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Insulin is a protein hormone. Its structure is maintained by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 9 — Biomolecules, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Insulin has two chains (A and B) connected by disulphide bonds between cysteine residues. These bonds maintain tertiary structure.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Insulin chains held by disulphide (-S-S-) bonds between cysteine residues.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The nitrogen cycle involves which of the following processes in correct order for conversion of N2 to NO3−?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.5
questions.ncert_line: N2 → NH3/NH4+ (nitrogen fixation by Rhizobium) → NO2− (nitrification by Nitrosomonas) → NO3− (by Nitrobacter).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. N2 → NH3 (fixation) → NO2− → NO3− (nitrification). Reverse = denitrification.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ozone depletion in the stratosphere is mainly caused by:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.4
questions.ncert_line: CFCs release Cl• radicals in stratosphere. Cl• catalytically destroys ozone: Cl• + O3 → ClO• + O2.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. CFCs → Cl• radicals → catalytic O3 destruction. One Cl• can destroy 1 lakh O3 molecules.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Eutrophication is caused by excess nutrients in water bodies. The immediate result is:

options.B: Algal bloom reducing oxygen for other organisms

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: Excess nutrients (N, P) → algal bloom → decomposition consumes O2 → hypoxia → death of aquatic organisms.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Eutrophication: nutrient excess → algal bloom → O2 depletion → aquatic death (hypoxia).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following is a viral disease?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 8 — Human Health and Disease, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: AIDS is caused by HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus). Typhoid = Salmonella (bacteria). Malaria = Plasmodium (protozoa).
questions.explanation: Correct: C. AIDS = HIV (retrovirus). HIV attacks CD4+ T-helper cells, collapsing immune system.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Biogas is produced by anaerobic digestion of organic matter. The main component of biogas is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Biogas is primarily methane (CH4, ~50-70%), CO2 (~30%), traces of H2S. Produced by methanogenic archaea.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Biogas = mainly CH4 (methane). Produced by methanogenic bacteria in anaerobic conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The oxidation state of Fe in K3[Fe(CN)6] is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2
questions.explanation: Correct: B. K3[Fe(CN)6]: Fe = +3. K4[Fe(CN)6] has Fe = +2. (Prussian blue involves both.)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes does NOT show geometrical isomerism?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: [Co(en)3]3+ has three identical bidentate ligands — no cis/trans isomerism possible. It only shows optical isomerism.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. [Co(en)3]3+ has no geometrical isomers (all ligands equivalent). It has optical isomers (Δ/Λ).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Carboxylic acids are stronger acids than alcohols because:

options.B: Carboxylate ion is stabilised by resonance
options.C: They are more soluble in water

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.5
questions.ncert_line: Carboxylate ion (RCOO⁻) has resonance stabilisation — negative charge delocalised over both oxygens. Alkoxide (RO⁻) has no such stabilisation.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RCOO⁻ stabilised by resonance → easier to lose H+ → stronger acid. pKa ~4-5 vs ~16 for alcohols.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aldol condensation requires an aldehyde or ketone with:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Aldol condensation requires α-hydrogen (H on carbon adjacent to carbonyl). Enolate ion attacks another carbonyl.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. α-hydrogen essential for aldol condensation. Benzaldehyde (no α-H) undergoes Cannizzaro reaction instead.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation polymerisation between:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 15 — Polymers, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Bakelite: phenol + formaldehyde → cross-linked polymer. First synthetic plastic. Thermosetting polymer.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Bakelite = phenol + formaldehyde. Thermosetting, cross-linked. Used in electrical switches.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: For the dissolution of a sparingly soluble salt AgCl, the solubility product Ksp = [Ag+][Cl−]. If Ksp = 1.8×10⁻¹⁰, the solubility of AgCl in pure water is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.13

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following transition metals does NOT show variable oxidation states?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Zinc has fully filled d orbitals (3d¹⁰) and shows only +2 oxidation state. No variable oxidation states like other transition metals.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn = only +2 (d¹⁰ fully filled). Fe = +2,+3. Cu = +1,+2. Mn = +2 to +7.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a parallel plate capacitor, if a dielectric slab is inserted between the plates while keeping the battery connected, which of the following INCREASES?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: Battery maintains constant V. Inserting dielectric: C = κε₀A/d increases. Q = CV → Q increases. E = V/d decreases.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Battery connected → V constant. C increases (dielectric) → Q = CV increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a potentiometer experiment, when the jockey is moved along the wire, the galvanometer shows zero deflection at a point. This point indicates:

options.B: Null point where EMF of cell = potential drop across wire length

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.15
questions.ncert_line: Null point in potentiometer: potential difference along wire length = EMF of cell being measured. No current through galvanometer.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Null point = balance point where galvanometer reads zero. EMF = φ × l (φ = potential gradient).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a series LCR circuit at resonance, the phase angle between current and voltage is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: At resonance XL = XC, so net reactance = 0. Z = R. Phase angle φ = tan⁻¹((XL−XC)/R) = tan⁻¹(0) = 0°.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. At resonance φ = 0° (current and voltage in phase). Circuit behaves as pure resistor.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a ray of light travels from a denser medium to a rarer medium at an angle greater than the critical angle:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Total internal reflection (TIR) occurs when angle of incidence > critical angle in denser medium.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. TIR: angle > critical angle → no refraction, complete reflection. Basis of optical fibre.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Rutherford's gold foil experiment, most alpha particles passed straight through because:

options.B: The nucleus is very small and most of the atom is empty space

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Most of atom is empty space — tiny dense nucleus at centre. Most alpha particles miss the nucleus and pass through.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Atom mostly empty space → most α particles undeflected. Few hit nucleus → large angle scattering.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg block slides down a frictionless incline of height 5 m. Its speed at the bottom is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Energy conservation: mgh = ½mv² → v = √(2gh) = √(2×10×5) = √100 = 10 m/s.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Primary productivity is highest in which of the following ecosystems?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.2
questions.ncert_line: Tropical rainforests have the highest net primary productivity due to year-round sunlight, high temperatures, and abundant rainfall.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Tropical rainforest = highest NPP (~2000 g/m²/yr). Desert = lowest (<200 g/m²/yr).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Decomposers in an ecosystem primarily break down:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 14 — Ecosystem, Section 14.4
questions.ncert_line: Decomposers (bacteria and fungi) break down dead organic matter (detritus) into inorganic nutrients, completing nutrient cycles.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Decomposers = break down detritus. Also called saprotrophs or saprobes.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The full form of BOD is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: BOD = Biochemical Oxygen Demand. It measures the amount of dissolved oxygen consumed by biological organisms in water.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. BOD = Biochemical Oxygen Demand. High BOD = heavily polluted water (more microbes breaking down organic matter).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Eutrophication of water bodies is caused by excessive:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 16 — Environmental Issues, Section 16.2
questions.ncert_line: Eutrophication: excess N and P from agricultural runoff causes algal blooms → algae die → decomposition depletes O2 → aquatic life dies.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Eutrophication caused by N and P (fertiliser runoff). Leads to algal bloom and oxygen depletion.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Carrying capacity (K) in logistic growth is defined as:

options.B: Maximum population size that an environment can support

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13 — Organisms and Populations, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Carrying capacity K = maximum population size the environment can sustain with available resources.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. K = carrying capacity. At K, dN/dt = 0 (no net growth). Logistic equation: dN/dt = rN(K-N)/K.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The bacterium used in the production of biogas (methane) is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Methanobacterium (methanogens) are anaerobic bacteria that produce methane (biogas) from organic matter.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Methanobacterium produces methane (CH4) in anaerobic conditions. These are archaebacteria.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Primary and secondary sewage treatment differs in that secondary treatment:

options.B: Uses biological treatment with microorganisms to reduce BOD

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 10 — Microbes in Human Welfare, Section 10.5
questions.ncert_line: Secondary treatment uses activated sludge (aerobic microorganisms) to break down dissolved organic matter, greatly reducing BOD.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Secondary (biological) treatment reduces BOD significantly. Primary = physical settling only.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following ions is colourless?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Zn²⁺ has electronic configuration [Ar]3d¹⁰ — fully filled d orbitals, no d-d transitions possible → colourless.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Zn²⁺ (d¹⁰) is colourless. d-d transitions need partially filled d orbitals for colour.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The magnetic moment of a transition metal ion with 3 unpaired electrons is approximately:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 8 — The d and f Block Elements, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: μ = √n(n+2) BM where n = number of unpaired electrons. n=3: μ = √(3×5) = √15 ≈ 3.87 BM.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. μ = √(n(n+2)) = √15 ≈ 3.87 BM for 3 unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: In the complex [Fe(CN)6]³⁻, the oxidation state of Fe is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.2
questions.ncert_line: CN⁻ is −1 each. Total charge of complex = −3. Fe + 6(−1) = −3. Fe = −3 + 6 = +3.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Fe + 6(−1) = −3 → Fe = +3. Compare [Fe(CN)6]⁴⁻ where Fe = +2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following complexes will show geometrical isomerism?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds, Section 9.6
questions.ncert_line: [Pt(NH3)2Cl2] is square planar with two different ligands — shows cis (same side) and trans (opposite) isomers.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Square planar MA2B2 → cis and trans isomers. cis-platin is the cis isomer (cancer drug).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Reverse osmosis involves applying pressure:

options.A: Less than osmotic pressure on solvent side
options.B: Greater than osmotic pressure on solution side

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 2 — Solutions, Section 2.4
questions.ncert_line: RO: external pressure greater than osmotic pressure applied on solution side forces pure solvent through semi-permeable membrane.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RO applies pressure > osmotic pressure on solution side → pure water passes to solvent side. Used in water purification.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following processes has ΔS > 0?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 6 — Thermodynamics, Section 6.4
questions.ncert_line: Sublimation: solid → gas. Huge increase in disorder (entropy). ΔS >> 0. All others involve decrease in randomness.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Sublimation increases entropy greatly (solid→gas). All phase changes to more disordered state have ΔS > 0.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Buffer solutions resist change in pH because they contain:

options.A: A strong acid and its salt
options.B: A weak acid and its conjugate base (or weak base and its conjugate acid)
options.C: Equal concentrations of acid and base
options.D: A strong base and its salt

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Chemistry, Chapter 7 — Equilibrium, Section 7.12
questions.ncert_line: Buffer = weak acid + conjugate base (or weak base + conjugate acid). Added H⁺ reacts with conjugate base; added OH⁻ reacts with weak acid.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Buffer contains weak acid + conjugate base. Henderson-Hasselbalch: pH = pKa + log([A⁻]/[HA]).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following polymers is a natural polymer?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 15 — Polymers, Section 15.1
questions.ncert_line: Cellulose is a natural polymer of glucose (β-1,4 linkage). Nylon, Bakelite, PVC are all synthetic polymers.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Cellulose = natural polymer (from plants). Starch, rubber, proteins are also natural polymers.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A train 100 m long travelling at 72 km/h crosses a bridge 1 km long. Time to cross completely is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Speed = 72 km/h = 20 m/s. Total distance = 1000 + 100 = 1100 m. Time = 1100/20 = 55 s.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Distance = bridge + train length = 1100 m. Time = 1100/20 = 55 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 5 kg on a frictionless surface is pulled by a force of 15 N. The acceleration is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: F = ma → a = F/m = 15/5 = 3 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The second law of thermodynamics states that heat cannot spontaneously flow from:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.6
questions.ncert_line: Second law: heat cannot spontaneously flow from a cold body to a hot body without external work (Clausius statement).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Heat flows spontaneously from hot→cold, never cold→hot (without work). This defines arrow of time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A spring-mass system has time period T. If the spring constant is doubled and mass is halved, new time period is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 14 — Oscillations, Section 14.4

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Beats are produced when two sound waves have:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.7
questions.ncert_line: Beats = periodic rise and fall in intensity. Produced by superposition of two waves with slightly different frequencies.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Beat frequency = |f1 − f2|. Audible beats when difference < 10 Hz.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a dielectric is inserted between capacitor plates (battery disconnected), which quantity increases?

options.D: Capacitance and charge stored remain same; energy decreases

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: Battery disconnected → Q constant. Inserting dielectric: C increases, V = Q/C decreases, E = V/d decreases, U = Q²/2C decreases.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Battery off: Q fixed. Dielectric → C↑, V↓, E↓, U↓. Energy released → dielectric pulled in.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The net magnetic moment of an antiferromagnetic material is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.5
questions.ncert_line: Antiferromagnetic: adjacent magnetic moments are anti-parallel and equal → net magnetic moment = 0.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Antiferromagnetic = zero net moment (anti-parallel moments cancel). Example: MnO, Cr.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Prokaryotic cells are generally smaller than eukaryotic cells. Which of the following is a correct reason?

options.B: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles, allowing simpler smaller organisation
options.C: Prokaryotes divide faster so stay small

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.2
questions.ncert_line: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles and nucleus. This simpler organisation permits a smaller cell size.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. No membrane-bound organelles = simpler organisation = smaller size. Eukaryotes need organelles for compartmentalisation.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The middle lamella between adjacent plant cells is mainly composed of:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 8 — Cell: The Unit of Life, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Middle lamella = calcium pectate (pectin). It cements adjacent cells together. First layer formed at cell plate during cytokinesis.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Middle lamella = calcium pectate. Primary wall = cellulose. Secondary wall = cellulose + lignin.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which of the following statements about RuBisCO is correct?

options.B: It has both carboxylase and oxygenase activity
options.C: It is found only in C4 plants

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 13 — Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: RuBisCO (ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase) can both fix CO2 (carboxylase) and react with O2 (oxygenase → photorespiration).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RuBisCO has dual activity. Oxygenase activity causes photorespiration — wasteful process in C3 plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Which plant hormone promotes cell elongation and is responsible for apical dominance?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Auxin (IAA) promotes cell elongation and causes apical dominance — apical bud suppresses lateral bud growth.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Auxin = cell elongation + apical dominance. Cytokinin promotes lateral bud growth (breaks apical dominance).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Ethylene gas promotes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Biology, Chapter 15 — Plant Growth and Development, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: Ethylene promotes fruit ripening, leaf abscission, senescence and breaking of seed dormancy. Apical dominance is promoted by auxin.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ethylene does NOT promote apical dominance — that's auxin. Ethylene promotes senescence and ripening.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The edible part of coconut (coconut water + flesh) represents:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 2 — Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Section 2.6
questions.ncert_line: Coconut water = free nuclear endosperm (liquid). Coconut flesh = cellular endosperm. Both are endosperm tissue.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Coconut water and flesh = endosperm. Endosperm provides nutrition for developing embryo.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The correct sequence of events in spermatogenesis is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 3 — Human Reproduction, Section 3.2
questions.ncert_line: Spermatogenesis: Spermatogonia (2n) → Primary spermatocyte (2n) → Secondary spermatocyte (n) → Spermatid (n) → Sperm (n).
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Spermatogonia → Primary/Secondary spermatocyte → Spermatid → Sperm.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: The Ti plasmid used in plant genetic engineering is obtained from:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 11 — Biotechnology: Principles and Processes, Section 11.4
questions.ncert_line: Ti plasmid (tumor-inducing) from Agrobacterium tumefaciens is a natural vector for introducing genes into dicot plants.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ti plasmid from Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Used as natural genetic engineer of dicot plants.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: Golden Rice is genetically modified to produce:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.2
questions.ncert_line: Golden Rice was engineered to produce beta-carotene (provitamin A) in the endosperm to address Vitamin A deficiency.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Golden Rice produces beta-carotene. Addresses Vitamin A deficiency in developing countries.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.question: RNA interference (RNAi) is used in biotechnology to:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Biology, Chapter 12 — Biotechnology and its Applications, Section 12.3
questions.ncert_line: RNAi uses double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) complementary to target mRNA → mRNA degradation → gene silencing.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. RNAi = gene silencing using dsRNA. Used in nematode control in tobacco (Meloidogyne).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Williamson synthesis is used to prepare:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 11 — Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers, Section 11.5
questions.ncert_line: Williamson synthesis: alkoxide + alkyl halide → ether. R-O⁻Na⁺ + R'X → R-O-R' + NaX.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Williamson synthesis → ethers. Best method for unsymmetrical ethers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Aldol condensation requires:

options.A: An aldehyde or ketone with alpha-hydrogen and base catalyst

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Aldol condensation: carbonyl compound with at least one α-hydrogen + dilute base → β-hydroxy carbonyl compound.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Needs α-H and base. Formaldehyde (no α-H) undergoes Cannizzaro reaction instead.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Which of the following undergoes Cannizzaro reaction?
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 12 — Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids, Section 12.4
questions.ncert_line: Cannizzaro reaction occurs with aldehydes lacking α-H (HCHO, C6H5CHO). Disproportionation gives acid + alcohol.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. HCHO has no α-H → Cannizzaro reaction (disproportionation). 2HCHO → HCOOH + CH3OH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) is specific for:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 13 — Amines, Section 13.3
questions.ncert_line: Carbylamine reaction: primary amine + CHCl3 + KOH → isocyanide (foul smell). Specific test for primary amines.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Carbylamine test (isocyanide formation) is specific for primary amines only.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: Nucleophilic substitution in haloarenes is much more difficult than in haloalkanes because:

options.A: C-X bond in arene is longer
options.B: C-X bond has partial double bond character due to resonance making it stronger
options.D: Halogen atoms in arenes are more electronegative

questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 10 — Haloalkanes and Haloarenes, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: In haloarenes, lone pair of halogen delocalises into benzene ring → C-X has partial double bond character → stronger → harder to cleave.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. C-X bond in arene is strengthened by resonance (partial double bond character) → less reactive to nucleophiles.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The anomeric carbon in glucose is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.2
questions.ncert_line: Anomeric carbon = C-1 in glucose (the carbonyl carbon in open chain form). Alpha and beta anomers differ at C-1.
questions.explanation: Correct: A. C-1 is anomeric carbon. α-D-glucose has OH below ring; β-D-glucose has OH above ring at C-1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question: The enzyme that catalyses the hydrolysis of peptide bonds is classified as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Chemistry, Chapter 14 — Biomolecules, Section 14.3
questions.ncert_line: Proteases (peptidases) are hydrolases that catalyse hydrolysis of peptide bonds (C-N bonds) in proteins.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Proteases = hydrolases. Peptide bond hydrolysis: R-CO-NH-R' + H2O → R-COOH + H2N-R'.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 10 m/s collides with a wall and bounces back at 8 m/s. Change in momentum is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.6
questions.ncert_line: Δp = m(v_f − v_i) = 2 × (−8 − 10) = 2 × (−18) = −36 kg m/s. Magnitude = 36 kg m/s.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. |Δp| = m|v_f − v_i| = 2 × 18 = 36 kg m/s (taking original direction as positive).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Power is defined as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.7
questions.ncert_line: Power P = W/t (average) = dW/dt = F·v (instantaneous). Both definitions are valid.
questions.explanation: Correct: D. P = W/t = F·v. Both are correct definitions of power.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The orbital period of a satellite at height h above Earth's surface depends on:

options.B: Radius of orbit (R+h) only (and Earth's mass)

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.6
questions.ncert_line: T = 2π√((R+h)³/GM). Period depends on orbital radius (R+h) and Earth's mass M — not satellite mass.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. T = 2π√(r³/GM). Independent of satellite mass. (Kepler's 3rd law: T² ∝ r³.)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Ohm's law is NOT valid for:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5
questions.ncert_line: Semiconductor diode has non-linear I-V characteristic — Ohm's law (linear I-V) does not apply.
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Diode: non-ohmic (non-linear I-V). Metals, electrolytes, resistors are approximately ohmic.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnifying power of a simple microscope with focal length f, for image at near point D is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.10
questions.ncert_line: Magnifying power of simple microscope = 1 + D/f (when image is at near point D = 25 cm).
questions.explanation: Correct: B. m = 1 + D/f for image at near point. m = D/f for image at infinity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The energy of hydrogen atom in the ground state (n=1) is −13.6 eV. Energy in n=3 state is:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a p-n junction diode under forward bias:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.6
questions.ncert_line: Forward bias reduces potential barrier → majority carriers flow easily → low resistance → large current.
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Forward bias: potential barrier reduced → large current. Reverse bias: barrier increased → negligible current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Young's modulus has the same dimensions as:
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Young's modulus = Stress/Strain. Strain is dimensionless, so Y has same dimensions as stress = Pa = N/m².
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Y = stress/strain. Since strain is dimensionless, Y has dimensions of stress (Pa).
